title: Hirunika’s Letter to Gotabaya After the Presidential Pardon of Duminda Silva
body_lines: In what has been described as a historic  ruling, the Sri Lankan Supreme Court  on May 31st 2022, issued an Interim Order suspending the operation of the Presidential Pardon granted by  President  Gotabaya Rajapaksa  to Arumadura Lawrence Romelo Duminda Silva known as Duminda Silva. The former parliamentarian  and ex- provincial councillor had been convicted  on a charge of murder and sentenced to death. After  being pardoned in June 2021 , Duminda Silva  was appointed in July  as Chairman of the National Housing Development Authority by President Rajapaksa. Earlier  during the time of  President  Mahinda  Rajapaksa, Duminda  Silva served as Monitoring MP of the Defence Ministry while Gotabaya was  the Defence  Ministry Secretary.

Duminda Silva  was involved in a shooting incident on October  8 2011, during Local Authority Elections, where former MP Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra  known as “Lucky Aiya”  and three of his bodyguards were killed.The High Court of Sri Lanka convicted Duminda Silva and four of his associates for murder and imposed the death sentence on them on September 16, 2016. Subsequently the sentence was appealed but a five-Judge bench of the Supreme Court upheld the murder conviction of Silva in a unanimous 51 page verdict issued on  October 16 , 2018. Despite the High Court conviction of 2016 being upheld by the Supreme Court in 2018, Duminda Silva was a free man in  June 2021.

Duminda Silva was the fortunate recipient of a munificent Presidential pardon on Poson Poya day. President Nandasena Gotabaya Rajapaksa invoking article 34(1) of the Constitution, granted a Presidential pardon to 93 convicted prisoners on Poson Poya day of 24 June  2021. Of these 16 were Tamil political prisoners charged and convicted under the draconian Prevention of Terrorism Act (PTA). Of the 77 other prisoners, 76 were people who had been serving sentences for relatively minor offences. These pardons and releases (except for one) did not create any controversy. Murder convict Duminda Silva’s pardon and release from death row  however caused much controversy.

There were many brickbats from various quarters ranging from former Justice Minister Thalatha Athukorale to US Ambassador Alaina. B. Teplitz. The Bar Association of Sri Lanka (BASL) in a detailed statement condemned the Presidential pardon for convict and former MP Duminda Silva and stated it was “unreasonable and arbitrary”.

The Bar Association of Sri Lanka (BASL)  wrote a letter  to President Rajapaksa that sought clarification on  specified issues and released the missive to the media. Among issues raised by the  BASL  were
(a) The basis on which Duminda Silva was selected for the purpose of granting a pardon under Article 34 (1) of the Constitution;
(b) The circumstances which were taken into consideration in the granting of such pardon;
(c) The reasons as to why the case of Duminda Silva stands out from others who are currently sentenced;

(d) Whether a report was called for by His Excellency the President from the Trial Judges as required by the Proviso to Article 34 (1) prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so the contents of the report;
(e) Whether the advice of the Attorney General was called for prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so the contents of such advice;
(f) Whether the recommendation of the Minister of Justice was obtained prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so whether the Minister of Justice made such a recommendation;

“The Bar Association of Sri Lanka maintains that if any one or more considerations stated above, were not satisfied in the current case, the pardon granted to Duminda Silva would be unreasonable and arbitrary and will result in erosion to the rule of law and result in a loss of public confidence in respect of the administration of justice” stated the BASL letter to  President Rajapaksa.

Following  the presidential pardon of Duminda Silva,  the victim Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra’s wdow  Sumana Premachandra,  daughter and Former Parliamentarian Hirunika Premachandra,  along  with  former Commissioner of Human Rights Commission of Sri Lanka (HRCSL) Ghazali Hussain PC, filed  Fundamental rights petitions seeking a declaration that the presidential pardon of Duminda Silva was invalid in law. The petitioners sought a declaration  from court, that their Fundamental Rights guaranteed under Articles 10, 11, 12 and 12(1) of the Constitution have been violated.

After preliminary hearings, a  three judge bench of the Supreme Court comprising Justice Preethi Padman Surasena, Justice Yasantha Kodagoda and Justice Achala Wengappuli issued  the Interim Order suspending the operation of the Presidential Pardon  The Supreme Court granted leave to proceed with the three Fundamental Rights petitions in terms of Article 12(1) of the constitution and  fixed the petitions to be taken up for argument on September 1, 2022. The interim order  suspending the Presidential  pardon will  remain  effective until the final determination of the three FR petitions.

Pursuant to the  Interim order, the  Supreme  Court ordered   the CID to place Duminda Silva under the custody of  the  Commissioner -General of Prisons  with immediate effect.The Attorney General was directed to assist the CID to implement this interim order while reporting to court the progress of the implementation of the Interim Order.The   Supreme Court also issued an Interim Order impounding Duminda Silva’s passport.

Attorney – General Sanjay Rajaratnam wrote to the Inspector General of Police (IGP) informing him of the SC’s interim orders which also included a directive  preventing Duminda Silva from leaving the country and impounding his passport.The AG informed the IGP that the interim orders be enforced through the Director of the Criminal Investigation Department.

Meanwhile the Chairman of the National Housing Development Authority, Duminda Silva  had been admiited  to the Sri Jayewardenepura hospital for medical treatment. Consequent to the SC interim order, the CID took him  arrested  Silva at the Neurological ward of the hospital. He was placed under special guard. Subsequently  Duminda Silva was transported to Prison.

Attorney-at-law Eraj De Silva with Daminda Wijerathne and Sundramoorthy Janagan under the instructions of Attorney-at-law Dimuthu Kuruppuarachchi appeared for Sumana Premachandra. M.A. Sumanthiran PC appeared for Hirunika Premachandra  while Jeffry Alagaratnam PC Ghazali Hussain.

Additional Solicitor General Nerin Pulle appeared for the Attorney General. President’s Counsel Gamini Marapana with Navin Marapana PC appeared for Duminda Silva. K. Kanag Iswaran PC instructed by Senior Counsel G.G. Arulpragasam appeared for the Bar Association of Sri Lanka, a respondent party to the petitions.

The SC interim order has been hailed widely as a landmark ruling. Although an interim order, the Supreme Court has through  the ruling ,   called into question, the Constitutional prerogative of the Executive  President  in granting  a pardon. The AG’s position that the court has no dominion over the grant of a pardon, does not seem to  have  resulted in the customary exercise of judicial restraint. In that sense,the SC ruling is truly  a historic landmark. However it is only an interim order  for now and what transpires in the future  – as further hearings  continue – remains to be seen.

The interim order resulting in the suspension of the presidential pardon and re-imprisonment of Duminda Silva has once again turned the media spotlight on the chief victim   Bharatha Lakshman’s  courageous daughter Hirunika Premachandra. The former MP has earned the sympathy and admiration of many people by her doughty campaign  for justice and against injustice.  Although the “Gota Go Home”protest has spread far and wide in recent times,it must be remembered that it was Hirunika along with brave women comrades who started it all.They took the fight into the lion’s den by pioneering the protest demonstration at the President’s private residence in Mirihana.

When President Gotabaya Rajapaksa pardoned her father’s convicted killer on  Poson Poya day last year, Hirunika Premachandra wrote an emotional letter in Sinhala  to the executive president.  The “Colombo Telegraph” website published an English translation. The insightful letter  encapsules the essence of what happened before and after  the killing of her father.More importantly, it is somewhat prophetic  when viewed against the backdrop  of the current anti-Rajapaksa agitation. I shall conclude this article by re-producing that letter in full –
President of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka Presidential Secretariat
Re: Granting Presidential Pardon to R. Dumunda Silva, who was convicted for the murder of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra  by seven
I am the daughter of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra, Trade Union Adviser to your brother, former President Mahinda Rajapaksa, and former Member of Parliament.

On October 8, 2011, my beloved father was shot and killed in broad daylight, his body riddled with over 20 bullets. Two High Court judges convicted former MP R Duminda Silva of his murder, and their ruling was upheld by five judges of the Supreme Court, including the Chief Justice. Today, you granted him a presidential pardon.

On that fateful day, my mother and I received an anonymous telephone call informing us that my father had been shot. We did not panic initially, because we were used such phone calls. In the past they had always been followed up with confirmation that my father was safe from harm.

In 1999, it was my father who organized the final election rally for President Chandrika Bandaranaike Kumaratunga in Borella. After the LTTE bomb attack on that rally, my father reached out to our family on the telephone to confirm that he was unharmed in the blast. Every morning, my brother and I would worship our father before leaving for school. I vividly remember that when we walked up to him to pay our respects the morning after the bomb blast at the rally, his tunic was still smeared with blood.

In a war-torn country, these incidents were common during his political career that spanned 30 years. But that day in October 2011, the phone never stopped ringing. My mother and I decided to leave for the National Hospital in Colombo. When we got there, we were told to make our way to the mortuary.
Ten years have passed, and the memory of my father’s tragic death still crushes me, filling me with dread and making me feel like the whole world is collapsing around me.

I was 23 years old when my father was assassinated. My mother and I, who had never stepped into a courtroom or a police station during his lifetime, trekked from courtroom to courtroom for five long years, to seek justice for my father.

The 20s in any young girl’s life, is a time for happiness and playfulness. I spent those years locking horns with a group of powerful, brutal murderers. When he died, hardly anybody knew Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra had a daughter. After his death, the whole country knew my name overnight. I made terrible enemies. All because I decided to pursue justice in court against my father’s killers.

When the Rajapaksa regime was in power, Duminda Silva did not spend a second in jail. This was the man the highest court has said led my father’s assassins and murdered people in broad daylight. It was the CID Director Shani Abeysekera and his officers who conducted meticulous investigations into my father’s murder, collecting every shred of evidence in an orderly and unimpeachable way. Today, Shani Abeysekera suffers for his courage to execute his duties as a police officer independently.
From the moment my father was killed, politicians across party lines flocked to pay their final respects to him. But you never showed up.

Mahinda Rajapaksa, Chamal Rajapaksa, Basil Rajapaksa, Shiranthi Rajapaksa, Namal Rajapaksa, Yoshitha Rajapaksa and Rohitha Rajapaksa all your family members attended my father’s funeral. But still you never appeared. You remained at Duminda Silva’s bedside at the Sri Jayewardenepura Hospital. Your priority was to order a helicopter to fly Duminda Silva to Singapore.

Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra entered politics in 1977, under the guidance of the late T.B. Illangaratne. Subsequently, he engaged in political activities with Vijaya Kumaratunga, as a founding member of Sri Lanka Mahajana Pakshaya. Entering parliament as a member representing the Sri Lanka Mahajana Pakshaya in 1994, he became a member of the Sri Lanka Freedom Party, under President Chandrika Bandaranaike Kumaratunga in 1996. This was at a time when barely a handful of people could be assembled to hoist a blue flag. At times, he fought single-handedly for the SLFP in the Colombo District.

From the day they met, my father developed a close friendship with your brother Mahinda Rajapaksa. For this reason, my father would refer to him only as “Mahinda aiyya”. Every step of the way, through joy and sorrow, my father stood with Mahinda Rajapaksa.

It was my father who battled against President Chandrika Kumaratunge’s decision to give the post of Leader of the Opposition to another person. It was Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra who fought for Mahinda Rajapaksa to be appointed prime minister when the leadership had decided otherwise. Any current politician in the SLPP who once held membership in the SLFP, knows this story.

You can ask anyone of them, irrespective if they are parliamentarians or not. You can learn this history from the Prime Minister himself. He was completely dedicated to his friend Mahinda who he considered a brother. Where he believed he was standing on principle, my father would not budge. He raised his voice continuously for laborers and marginalized people at the grassroots level. He did not amass wealth as a politician. Instead, he acted as a friend and brother to everyone in the government and opposition.
But he made one misstep on a public stage a few days before he was killed.

“The Organizer of Kolonnawa, is taking the Defense Secretary who is the President’s brother, to every house to which he is selling Heroin. This is my worry. I must state at this point that we will not only ensure the victory of Prasanna. We will also fight to sever the Rajapaksa generation we built with much difficulty, from these heroin dealers.”
I believe that these few words may have led to his murder. These are the very last words my father uttered in public.
Mr. President, ask yourself how prophetic these words of a murdered man have proved today.

A decade later, my father’s brutal death still haunts me. From time to time, I have had to seek treatment to deal with the pain and trauma. As a mother of three, I try hard to be optimistic and move forward. But to this day, the loss of my father cuts through that joy and fills me with unbearable pain. Perhaps this is how all families  suffer the sudden, tragic death of a loved one. The families of Prageeth Eknaligoda, Lasantha Wickrematunge and Wasim Thajudeen – they must all be living through this trauma every day of their lives. No matter how full our lives are, what blessings we receive, there is a void in our hearts that will never be filled.
These victims and their families have never been given justice. They have a right to see these killers brought to court and face up to their crimes.
You campaigned and won the presidential election under the these ‘One Country, One Law’. Today, those words have been reduced to nothing more than sloganeering to win an election.

Now, if every prisoner on death row demands a presidential pardon, you are in no position to refuse. If convicted prisoners are to be arbitrarily released by presidential pardon, what are prisons for? Why do we have laws? Why do we have judges?

By your cowardly decisions, today, this country is lawless. This is a nation of slaves, in the grip of a master leading them to certain death. You are not the real ruler of this country today. You must accept that. You are just a puppet manipulated by other forces.

Believing in the vision of you as a leader like Mahathir Mohamed, Lee Kuan Yew or Vladimir Putin, 6.9 million Sri Lankan citizens placed their faith in you and propelled you to the presidency. They gave you a two thirds majority in Parliament. They made it possible for you to enact the 20th Amendment to the Constitution.

What more tools could you possibly need to make difficult decisions that will benefit all Sri Lankans. Instead, you have chosen to become a puppet. It is impossible to imagine now that your rule will ever usher in a better country. Your actions in this past year have amply demonstrated that.

My father is now a footnote in history.  But he has left behind so much for his grandchildren to take pride in and remember. What legacy do you hope to leave behind? What will  you leave behind for your newborn granddaughter to speak of proudly when you are gone?

In accordance with the Buddhist precepts that I live by, I have already forgiven former MP Duminda Silva. But Buddhism also teaches that rulers must be just and uphold the laws of the land. Your actions have undermined the rule of law and made a mockery of justice.

Two out of three High Court judges ruled that Duminda Silva had murdered my father and sentenced the killers to death. The defendants appealed to the Supreme Court. Five judges of the Supreme Court including the then Chief Justice, upheld the verdict of the High Court. Five judges of the Supreme Court determined that Duminda Silva had been the ringleader of the illegal assembly that killed my father. If the decisions of High Court and Supreme Court judges are cast into the garbage and the final decisions are made by you, of what use are laws in this country?

6.9 million people put their faith in you and chose you to be leader of this country. You must now be prepared to answer them. Your present conduct makes sure that the people will never allow a Rajapaksa to rule the country again.

Now that you have dismissed the verdict of seven judges to release Duminda Silva, I would not be surprised if you were to nominate him as a Chief Minister and bring him back into politics. This country is transformed now into a nation of slaves, being marshalled towards their own destruction by their masters. But the people will not tolerate injustice forever.

Your rule is breeding injustice, Mr President, and the day is not far off when the people will break these shackles and rise up. When it comes, people like you who have no backbone and no respect for justice and the rule of law will be bereft of a political future.
This is an updated version of an article written for the “Political Pulse” Column appearing in the “Daily FT” of June 8th , 2022. It can be accessed here:

si.title: දුමින්ද සිල්වාට ජනාධිපති සමාව ලැබීමෙන් පසු හිරුණිකා ගෝඨාභයට ලිපියක්
body_lines: ඉතිහාසගත තීන්දුවක් ලෙස විස්තර කර ඇති පරිදි, ශ්‍රී ලංකා ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය 2022 මැයි 31 වන දින, දුමින්ද සිල්වා නමින් හැඳින්වෙන අරුමදුර ලෝරන්ස් රොමෙලෝ දුමින්ද සිල්වාට ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ විසින් ලබා දුන් ජනාධිපති සමාව ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීම අත්හිටුවමින් අතුරු නියෝගයක් නිකුත් කළේය. හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී සහ හිටපු පළාත් සභා මන්ත්‍රීවරයා මිනීමැරුම් චෝදනාවකට වරදකරු වී මරණ දණ්ඩනයට නියම වී ඇත. 2021 ජූනි මස සමාව ලැබීමෙන් පසු දුමින්ද සිල්වා ජනාධිපති රාජපක්ෂ විසින් ජාතික නිවාස සංවර්ධන අධිකාරියේ සභාපති ලෙස ජූලි මාසයේදී පත් කරන ලදී. මීට පෙර මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ ජනාධිපතිවරයාගේ කාලයේ දුමින්ද සිල්වා ආරක්ෂක අමාත්‍යාංශයේ අධීක්ෂණ මන්ත්‍රීවරයා ලෙස කටයුතු කළ අතර ගෝඨාභය ආරක්ෂක අමාත්‍යාංශයේ ලේකම්වරයා ලෙස කටයුතු කළේය.

දුමින්ද සිල්වා 2011 ඔක්තෝම්බර් 8 වන දින පළාත් පාලන මැතිවරණය අතරතුර වෙඩි තැබීමේ සිද්ධියකට සම්බන්ධ වූ අතර එහිදී හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර "ලකී අයියා" සහ ඔහුගේ ආරක්ෂකයින් තිදෙනෙකු ඝාතනය විය. ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහාධිකරණය දුමින්ද සිල්වා ඇතුළු සිව් දෙනෙකු වරදකරුවන් කළේය. ඔහුගේ සහචරයන් මිනීමැරුම් චෝදනාවට ඔවුන්ට මරණ දඬුවම නියම කළේ 2016 සැප්තැම්බර් 16 වැනිදාය. පසුව එම දඬුවම අභියාචනා කළ නමුත් ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණයේ පංච පුද්ගල විනිසුරු මඩුල්ලක් විසින් 2018 ඔක්තෝම්බර් 16 වැනි දින නිකුත් කරන ලද පිටු 51ක ඒකමතික තීන්දුවකින් සිල්වා ඝාතන දඬුවම තහවුරු කළේය. 2016 වසරේ මහාධිකරණයේ වරදකරු 2018 දී ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය විසින් තහවුරු කරනු ලැබුවද දුමින්ද සිල්වා 2021 ජුනි මාසයේදී නිදහස් මිනිසෙකි.

දුමින්ද සිල්වා මහතා පොසොන් පොහොය දින ජනාධිපති සමාව ලැබූ වාසනාවන්තයා විය. ජනාධිපති නන්දසේන ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ මහතා ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවේ 34(1) වගන්තිය ක්‍රියාවට නංවමින් 2021 ජුනි 24 පොසොන් පොහොය දින සිරකරුවන් 93 දෙනෙකුට ජනාධිපති සමාව ප්‍රදානය කළේය. මෙම 16 දෙනාගෙන් කුරිරු ත්‍රස්තවාදය වැලැක්වීමේ පනත (PTA) යටතේ චෝදනා ලැබ වරදකරුවන් වූ දෙමළ දේශපාලන සිරකරුවන්ය. ) අනෙකුත් සිරකරුවන් 77 දෙනාගෙන් 76 දෙනෙකුම සාපේක්ෂව සුළු වැරදි සඳහා සිරදඬුවම් විඳිමින් සිටි අයයි. මෙම සමාව සහ නිදහස් කිරීම් (එකක් හැර) කිසිදු මතභේදයක් ඇති කළේ නැත. කෙසේ වෙතත් ඝාතන චූදිත දුමින්ද සිල්වාට සමාව දී මරණ දඬුවමෙන් නිදහස් වීම බොහෝ ආන්දෝලනයකට තුඩු දුන්නේය.

හිටපු අධිකරණ ඇමැතිනී තලතා අතුකෝරළ මහත්මියගේ සිට ඇමෙරිකානු තානාපතිනී ඇලෙයිනා දක්වා විවිධ පාර්ශ්වවලින් ගඩොල් බැට්‌ටියෝ රැසක්‌ සිටියහ. බී. ටෙප්ලිට්ස්. ශ්‍රී ලංකා නීතීඥ සංගමය (BASL) සවිස්තරාත්මක ප්‍රකාශයක් කරමින් වැරදිකරු සහ හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී දුමින්ද සිල්වාට ජනාධිපති සමාව ලබා දීම හෙළා දකින අතර එය "අසාධාරණ සහ අත්තනෝමතික" බව ප්‍රකාශ කළේය.
ශ්‍රී ලංකා නීතීඥ සංගමය (BASL) ජනාධිපති රාජපක්ෂ වෙත ලිපියක් යවමින් නිශ්චිත කරුණු සම්බන්ධයෙන් පැහැදිලි කිරීමක් ඉල්ලා සිටි අතර එම පණිවිඩය මාධ්‍ය වෙත නිකුත් කළේය. BASL විසින් මතු කරන ලද ගැටළු අතර විය
(අ) ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවේ 34 (1) ව්‍යවස්ථාව යටතේ සමාව ලබා දීම සඳහා දුමින්ද සිල්වා තෝරා ගනු ලැබූ පදනම කවරේද;
(ආ) එම සමාව ලබා දීමේදී සැලකිල්ලට ගත් තත්ත්වයන් කවරේද;
(ඇ) දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ නඩුව දැනට දඬුවම් විඳිමින් සිටින අනෙකුත් අයගෙන් කැපී පෙනෙන්නේ මන්දැයි යන්නට හේතු කවරේද;
(ඈ) දුමින්ද සිල්වාට සමාව ලබා දීමට පෙර 34 (1) වගන්තියේ විධිවිධානයට අනුව අතිගරු ජනාධිපතිතුමා විසින් නඩු විභාගයේ විනිසුරුවරුන්ගෙන් වාර්තාවක් ඉල්ලා සිටියේද යන්න සහ එසේ නම් එම වාර්තාවේ අන්තර්ගතය;
(ඉ) දුමින්ද සිල්වාට සමාව ලබාදීමට පෙර නීතිපතිවරයාගේ උපදෙස් ඉල්ලා සිටියේද යන්න සහ එසේ නම් එම උපදෙස්වල අන්තර්ගතය කවරේද;
(ඊ) දුමින්ද සිල්වාට සමාව ලබාදීමට පෙර අධිකරණ අමාත්‍යවරයාගේ නිර්දේශය ලබා ගත්තේද සහ එසේ නම් අධිකරණ අමාත්‍යවරයා එවැනි නිර්දේශයක් කළේද;

"ඉහත සඳහන් කර ඇති කරුණු එකක් හෝ කිහිපයක් වත්මන් නඩුවේදී සෑහීමකට පත් නොවන්නේ නම්, දුමින්ද සිල්වාට ලබා දී ඇති සමාව අසාධාරණ හා අත්තනෝමතික වන අතර එය නීතියේ ආධිපත්‍යය ඛාදනය වීමට හේතු වන බව ශ්‍රී ලංකා නීතීඥ සංගමය පවසයි. යුක්තිය පසිඳලීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් මහජන විශ්වාසය නැතිවීම" යනුවෙන් ජනාධිපති රාජපක්ෂ වෙත BASL ලිපියේ සඳහන් විය.

දුමින්ද සිල්වා මහතාට ජනාධිපති සමාව ලැබීමෙන් පසු වින්දිත භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍රගේ බිරිඳ වන සුමනා ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර, දියණිය සහ හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රීනී හිරුණිකා ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර, ශ්‍රී ලංකා මානව හිමිකම් කොමිසමේ හිටපු කොමසාරිස් (HRCSL) ජනාධිපති නීතිඥ ගසාලි හුසේන් සමඟ එක්ව ප්‍රකාශයක් නිකුත් කරන ලෙස ඉල්ලා මූලික අයිතිවාසිකම් පෙත්සම් ගොනු කරන ලදී. දුමින්ද සිල්වාට දුන් ජනාධිපති සමාව නීතියෙන් වලංගු නැත. ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවේ 10, 11, 12 සහ 12(1) වගන්ති යටතේ සහතික කර ඇති තම මූලික අයිතිවාසිකම් කඩ වී ඇති බවට ප්‍රකාශයක් කරන ලෙස පෙත්සම්කරුවෝ අධිකරණයෙන් ඉල්ලා සිටියහ.

මූලික නඩු විභාගවලින් අනතුරුව විනිසුරු ප්‍රීති පද්මන් සූරසේන, විනිසුරු යසන්ත කෝදාගොඩ සහ අචල වෙංගප්පුලි යන ත්‍රිපුද්ගල ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණ විනිසුරු මඩුල්ල ජනාධිපති සමාව ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීම අත්හිටුවමින් අතුරු නියෝගය නිකුත් කළේය. ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවේ 12(1) වගන්තියේ නියමයන් සහ පෙත්සම් 2022 සැප්තැම්බර් 1 වැනි දින තර්කය සඳහා කැඳවීමට නියම කරන ලදී. ජනාධිපති සමාව අත්හිටුවීමේ අතුරු නියෝගය FR පෙත්සම් තුනේ අවසාන තීරණය දක්වා බලපැවැත්වේ.

එම අතුරු නියෝගයට අනුව දුමින්ද සිල්වා වහාම ක්‍රියාත්මක වන පරිදි බන්ධනාගාර කොමසාරිස් ජනරාල්වරයාගේ භාරයට පත් කරන ලෙස ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය රහස් පොලිසියට නියෝග කළේය. එහි ප්‍රගතිය අධිකරණයට වාර්තා කරමින් මෙම අතුරු නියෝගය ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීම සඳහා අපරාධ පරීක්ෂණ දෙපාර්තමේන්තුවට සහය වන ලෙස නීතිපතිවරයාට නියම කෙරිණි. අතුරු නියෝගය ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීම සම්බන්ධයෙන්. දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ විදේශ ගමන් බලපත්‍රය රාජසන්තක කරමින් ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය අතුරු නියෝගයක් ද නිකුත් කළා.

දුමින්ද සිල්වාට රටින් පිටවීම වළක්වා ඔහුගේ විදේශ ගමන් බලපත්‍රය රාජසන්තක කර ගැනීමේ නියෝගයක් ද ඇතුළත් ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණයේ අතුරු නියෝග පිළිබඳව දන්වමින් නීතිපති සංජය රාජරත්නම් පොලිස්පතිවරයාට ලිඛිතව දන්වා සිටියේය. අපරාධ පරීක්ෂණ දෙපාර්තමේන්තුවේ අධ්‍යක්ෂවරයා හරහා.

මේ අතර ජාතික නිවාස සංවර්ධන අධිකාරියේ සභාපති දුමින්ද සිල්වා වෛද්‍ය ප්‍රතිකාර සඳහා ශ්‍රී ජයවර්ධනපුර රෝහලට ඇතුළත් කර තිබුණා. ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණයේ අතුරු නියෝගය අනුව රහස් පොලිසිය ඔහුව රෝහලේ ස්නායු රෝග වාට්ටුවට රැගෙන ගොස් සිල්වා අත්අඩංගුවට ගත්තේය. ඔහු විශේෂ ආරක්ෂාවක් යටතේ තබා ඇත. පසුව දුමින්ද සිල්වා බන්ධනාගාරයට රැගෙන ගියා.

සුමනා ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මහතා වෙනුවෙන් නීතිඥ දිමුතු කුරුප්පුආරච්චි මහතාගේ උපදෙස්‌ මත දමින්ද විජේරත්න සහ සුන්දරමූර්ති ජනගන් සමඟ නීතිඥ එරාජ් ද සිල්වා මහතා පෙනී සිටියේය. ජනාධිපති නීතිඥ එම්.ඒ.සුමන්තිරන් හිරුණිකා ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර වෙනුවෙන් පෙනී සිටි අතර ජනාධිපති නීතිඥ ජෙෆ්රි අලගරත්නම් ගසාලි හුසේන් මහත්මිය පෙනී සිටියාය.

නීතිපතිවරයා වෙනුවෙන් අතිරේක සොලිසිටර් ජනරාල් නෙරින් පුල්ලේ මහතා පෙනී සිටියේය. දුමින්ද සිල්වා මහතා වෙනුවෙන් ජනාධිපති නීතිඥ ගාමිණී මාරපන මහතා සහ ජනාධිපති නීතිඥ නවීන් මාරපන මහතා පෙනී සිටියේය. ජනාධිපති නීතිඥ කේ. කනග් ඊශ්වරන් මහතාගේ උපදෙස් මත ජ්‍යෙෂ්ඨ නීතිඥ ජී.ජී. පෙත්සම්වල වගඋත්තරකාර පාර්ශ්වයක් වන ශ්‍රී ලංකා නීතිඥ සංගමය වෙනුවෙන් අරුල්ප්‍රගාසම් පෙනී සිටියේය.

SC අතුරු නියෝගය සුවිශේෂී තීන්දුවක් ලෙස පුළුල් ලෙස පිළිගෙන ඇත. අතුරු නියෝගයක් වුවද, ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය විසින් මෙම තීන්දුව හරහා විධායක ජනාධිපතිවරයාට සමාව ලබාදීමේ ව්‍යවස්ථාපිත වරප්‍රසාදය ප්‍රශ්න කර ඇත. සමාව ලබා දීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් අධිකරණයට ආධිපත්‍යයක් නොමැති බවට නීතිපතිවරයාගේ ස්ථාවරය, චාරිත්‍රානුකූලව අධිකරණ සීමාවන් ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීමට හේතු වී ඇති බවක් නොපෙනේ. ඒ අර්ථයෙන්, SC තීන්දුව සැබවින්ම ඓතිහාසික සන්ධිස්ථානයකි. කෙසේ වෙතත් එය දැනට අතුරු නියෝගයක් පමණක් වන අතර අනාගතයේදී සිදු වන්නේ කුමක්ද - වැඩිදුර විභාගයන් දිගටම පවතින බැවින් - දැකීමට ඉතිරිව ඇත.

දුමින්ද සිල්වා මහතාට ජනාධිපති සමාව ලබා දීම අත්හිටුවා යළි සිරගත කිරීමේ අතුරු නියෝගය යළිත් වරක් මාධ්‍ය අවධානය ප්‍රධාන වින්දිත භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන්ගේ දිරිය දියණිය හිරුණිකා ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර වෙත යොමු කර තිබේ. හිටපු මන්ත්‍රීවරිය යුක්තිය සහ අයුක්තියට එරෙහි සිය ඩෝසර් ව්‍යාපාරයෙන් බොහෝ දෙනෙකුගේ අනුකම්පාව සහ ප්‍රසාදයට පාත්‍ර වූවාය. “ගෝඨා ගෙදර යන්න” විරෝධතාව මෑත කාලයේ බොහෝ තැන්වල පැතිර ගියත් ඒ සියල්ල ආරම්භ කළේ හිරුණිකා සහ අභීත කාන්තා සහෝදරියන් බව මතක තබා ගත යුතුය. ඔවුන් එම සටන සිංහ ගුහාවට ගෙන ගියේ එම විරෝධතාවයේ පුරෝගාමීත්වයෙනි. මිරිහානේ පිහිටි ජනපතිගේ පෞද්ගලික නිවස.

පසුගිය වසරේ පොසොන් පොහෝ දින ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ තම පියාගේ ඝාතකයාට සමාව දුන් අවස්ථාවේදී හිරුණිකා ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර විධායක ජනාධිපතිවරයාට සිංහලෙන් සංවේදී ලිපියක් ලිව්වාය. “Colombo Telegraph” වෙබ් අඩවිය ඉංග්‍රීසි පරිවර්තනයක් පළ කළේය. තීක්ෂ්ණ බුද්ධියෙන් යුත් ලිපිය ඇගේ පියා ඝාතනය කිරීමට පෙර සහ පසුව සිදු වූ දේවල හරය කැටි කර ඇත. වඩා වැදගත් වන්නේ වත්මන් රාජපක්ෂ විරෝධී උද්ඝෝෂණයේ පසුබිමට එරෙහිව බලන විට එය තරමක් අනාවැකිමය වීමයි. එම ලිපිය සම්පූර්ණයෙන් නැවත ඉදිරිපත් කිරීමෙන් මම මෙම ලිපිය අවසන් කරමි -
ශ්‍රී ලංකා ප්‍රජාතාන්ත්‍රික සමාජවාදී ජනරජයේ සභාපති ජනාධිපති ලේකම් කාර්යාලය
Re: භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මහතා හත් දෙනෙකු විසින් ඝාතනය කිරීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් වරදකරු වූ ආර්.දුමුන්ද සිල්වාට ජනාධිපති සමාව ලබාදීම.
මම ඔබේ සහෝදරයා වන හිටපු ජනාධිපති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ මහතාගේ වෘත්තීය සමිති උපදේශක, හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මහතාගේ දියණියයි.

2011 ඔක්තෝම්බර් 8 වෙනිදා මගේ ආදරණීය තාත්තා මහ දවල් වෙඩි තියලා මරලා දැම්මා, උණ්ඩ 20 කට වැඩි ප්‍රමාණයක් ඔහුගේ සිරුරට වැදී තිබුණා. හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී ආර් දුමින්ද සිල්වා ඝාතනය සම්බන්ධයෙන් මහාධිකරණ විනිසුරුවරුන් දෙදෙනෙකු වරදකරු කළ අතර ඔවුන්ගේ තීන්දුව අගවිනිසුරුවරයා ඇතුළු ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණයේ විනිසුරුවරුන් පස් දෙනෙකු විසින් අනුමත කරන ලදී. අද ඔබ ඔහුට ජනාධිපති සමාව ලබා දුන්නා.

ඒ දෛවෝපගත දිනයේ මටත් මගේ මවටත් නිර්නාමික දුරකථන ඇමතුමක් මගින් තාත්තාට වෙඩි තබා ඇති බව දන්වා සිටියේය. අපි එවැනි දුරකථන ඇමතුම් භාවිතා කළ නිසා අපි මුලින් කලබල වුණේ නැහැ. අතීතයේදී ඔවුන් හැම විටම පසු විපරම් කළේ මගේ පියා අනතුරකින් ආරක්ෂා වී ඇති බවට තහවුරු කරමිනි.

1999 චන්ද්‍රිකා බණ්ඩාරනායක කුමාරතුංග ජනාධිපතිතුමිය වෙනුවෙන් බොරැල්ලේ අවසන් මැතිවරණ රැලිය සංවිධානය කළේ මගේ තාත්තා. එම රැලියට කොටි බෝම්බ ප්‍රහාරයෙන් පසු මගේ පියා අපේ පවුලට දුරකථනයෙන් කතා කළේ පිපිරීමෙන් තමාට හානියක් නොවන බව තහවුරු කර ගැනීමටය. හැමදාම උදේ ඉස්කෝලේ යන්න කලින් මමයි මල්ලියි තාත්තට වඳිනවා. රැලියේ බෝම්බ පිපිරවීමෙන් පසු දින උදෑසන අප ඔහුට ගෞරව දැක්වීමට ඔහු වෙත යන විටත් ඔහුගේ ඇඳුමේ ලේ තැවරී තිබූ බව මට හොඳින් මතකය.

යුද්ධයෙන් බැට කෑ රටක ඔහුගේ වසර 30ක දේශපාලන ජීවිතය තුළ මෙවැනි සිදුවීම් සුලභ විය. නමුත් 2011 ඔක්තෝම්බර් මාසයේදී දුරකථනය නාද වීම නතර වූයේ නැත. මමයි අම්මයි තීරණය කළා කොළඹ ජාතික රෝහලට යන්න. අපි එතනට ගියාම කිව්වා මළ ගෙදරට යන්න කියලා.
වසර දහයක් ගත වී ඇත, මගේ පියාගේ ඛේදජනක මරණය පිළිබඳ මතකය තවමත් මාව තලා දමයි, මා භීතියෙන් පුරවා, මුළු ලෝකයම මා වටා කඩා වැටෙන බවක් මට දැනේ.
මගේ පියා ඝාතනය වන විට මට වයස අවුරුදු 23 යි. ඔහුගේ ජීවිත කාලය තුළ කිසි දිනෙක උසාවියකට හෝ පොලිසියකට නොපැමිණි මගේ මව සහ මම, මගේ පියාට යුක්තිය පතා වසර පහක් තිස්සේ උසාවියෙන් උසාවියට ​​ඇවිද ගියෙමු.

ඕනෑම තරුණ ගැහැණු ළමයෙකුගේ ජීවිතයේ 20 ගණන්, සතුට හා සෙල්ලක්කාර කාලයකි. බලගතු, කුරිරු මිනීමරුවන් කණ්ඩායමක් සමඟ මම ඒ අවුරුදු ගණනාව ගත කළා. ඔහු මිය යන විට භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍රට දියණියක් සිටි බව කිසිවෙක් දැන සිටියේ නැත. ඔහුගේ මරණයෙන් පසු මුළු රටම එක රැයකින් මගේ නම දැනගත්තා. මම දරුණු සතුරන් ඇති කළෙමි. මගේ පියාගේ ඝාතකයන්ට එරෙහිව අධිකරණයේ යුක්තිය ඉටු කිරීමට මම තීරණය කළ නිසා.

රාජපක්ෂ රෙජීමය බලයේ සිටින විට දුමින්ද සිල්වා තත්පරයක්වත් හිරේ සිටියේ නැත. මගේ පියාගේ ඝාතකයන්ට නායකත්වය දී මහ දවාලේ මිනිසුන් ඝාතනය කළ බව ඉහළම උසාවිය ප්‍රකාශ කළ පුද්ගලයා මෙයයි. අපරාධ පරීක්ෂණ දෙපාර්තමේන්තුවේ අධ්‍යක්ෂ ශානි අබේසේකර සහ ඔහුගේ නිලධාරීන් තමයි මගේ පියාගේ ඝාතනය සම්බන්ධයෙන් සෑම සාක්ෂියක්ම පිළිවෙළකට සහ දෝෂාභියෝගයකට ලක් කළ නොහැකි ආකාරයට රැස්කරමින් ඉතා සූක්ෂම ලෙස පරීක්ෂණ කළේ. අද ශානි අබේසේකර පොලිස් නිලධාරියෙකු ලෙස තම රාජකාරිය ස්වාධීනව ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීමට දක්වන ධෛර්යය වෙනුවෙන් දුක් විඳියි.
මගේ පියා ඝාතනය කළ මොහොතේ සිට ඔහුට අවසන් ගෞරව දැක්වීමට පක්ෂ භේදයකින් තොරව දේශපාලනඥයෝ රොක් වූහ. නමුත් ඔබ කවදාවත් පෙනී සිටියේ නැත.

මහින්ද රාජපක්‍ෂ, චමල් රාජපක්‍ෂ, බැසිල් රාජපක්‍ෂ, ශිරන්ති රාජපක්‍ෂ, නාමල් රාජපක්‍ෂ, යෝෂිත රාජපක්‍ෂ, රෝහිත රාජපක්‍ෂ ඔබේ පවුලේ සියලුම දෙනා මගේ පියාගේ අවසන් කටයුතුවලට සහභාගි වුණා. නමුත් ඔබ තවමත් පෙනී සිටියේ නැත. ඔබ ශ්‍රී ජයවර්ධනපුර රෝහලේ දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ ඇඳ අසල නැවතී සිටියා. දුමින්ද සිල්වා සිංගප්පූරුවට ගෙන යාමට හෙලිකොප්ටරයක් ​​ඇණවුම් කිරීම ඔබේ ප්‍රමුඛතාවය විය.

භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර දේශපාලනයට පිවිසියේ 1977 දී දිවංගත ටී.බී. ඉලංගරත්න. ඉන්පසුව ඔහු ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහජන පක්ෂයේ ආරම්භක සාමාජිකයෙකු ලෙස විජය කුමාරතුංග සමඟ දේශපාලන කටයුතුවල නිරත විය. 1994 දී ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහජන පක්ෂය නියෝජනය කරමින් පාර්ලිමේන්තුවට පිවිසි ඔහු 1996 දී චන්ද්‍රිකා බණ්ඩාරනායක කුමාරතුංග ජනාධිපතිනිය යටතේ ශ්‍රී ලංකා නිදහස් පක්ෂයේ සාමාජිකත්වය ලබා ගත්තේ ය. නිල් කොඩිය. විටෙක ඔහු කොළඹ දිස්ත්‍රික්කයේ ශ්‍රී ලංකා නිදහස් පක්ෂය වෙනුවෙන් තනිව සටන් කළේය.

ඔවුන් මුණගැසුණු දින සිටම මගේ පියා ඔබේ සහෝදරයා වන මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ සමඟ සමීප මිත්‍රත්වයක් ගොඩනඟා ගත්තා. මේ හේතුව නිසා තාත්තා ඔහුව හඳුන්වන්නේ “මහින්ද අයියා” ලෙස පමණයි. ගමනේ සෑම පියවරකදීම සතුට හා දුක මැද තාත්තා සිටියේ මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ මහතා සමඟයි.

ජනාධිපති චන්ද්‍රිකා කුමාරතුංග මහත්මිය විපක්ෂ නායක ධුරය වෙනත් අයෙකුට ලබාදීමට ගත් තීරණයට එරෙහිව සටන් කළේ මගේ පියායි. නායකත්වය වෙනස් තීරණයක් ගෙන තිබියදී මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ අගමැති කරවීමට සටන් කළේ භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මහතාය. කලක් ශ්‍රීලනිපයේ සාමාජිකත්වය දැරූ ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණේ වත්මන් දේශපාලනඥයෙක් මේ කතාව දනී.

ඒ අයගෙන් ඕනම කෙනෙක්ගෙන් අහන්න පුළුවන්, ඔවුන් පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රීවරුද නැද්ද කියලා. මේ ඉතිහාසය අගමැතිතුමාගෙන්ම ඉගෙන ගන්න පුළුවන්. ඔහු සහෝදරයෙක් ලෙස සලකන තම මිත්‍ර මහින්ද වෙනුවෙන් සම්පූර්ණයෙන්ම කැපවී සිටියේය. ඔහු ප්‍රතිපත්තිමය මත සිටින බව ඔහු විශ්වාස කළ තැන, මගේ පියා නොසැලී සිටියේය. ඔහු කම්කරුවන් සහ බිම් මට්ටමේ ආන්තික ජනතාව වෙනුවෙන් අඛණ්ඩව තම හඬ අවදි කළේය. ඔහු දේශපාලකයෙකු ලෙස ධනය රැස් කළේ නැත. ඒ වෙනුවට ඔහු ක්‍රියා කළේ ආණ්‌ඩු පක්‍ෂයේ සහ විපක්‍ෂයේ සියලු දෙනාගේ හිතවතෙක්‌ සහ සහෝදරයෙක්‌ ලෙසයි.
නමුත් ඔහු මරා දැමීමට දින කිහිපයකට පෙර ප්‍රසිද්ධ වේදිකාවකදී එක් වැරදි පියවරක් ගත්තේය.

“කොලොන්නාවේ සංවිධායකතුමා ජනාධිපතිතුමාගේ සහෝදරයා වන ආරක්ෂක ලේකම්වරයා කුඩු විකුණන හැම ගෙදරකටම එක්කගෙන යනවා. මෙය මගේ කනස්සල්ලයි. අපි ප්‍රසන්නගේ ජයග්‍රහණය පමණක් තහවුරු නොකරන බව මම මේ අවස්ථාවේදී ප්‍රකාශ කළ යුතුයි. අපි බොහොම අමාරුවෙන් ගොඩනැගූ රාජපක්ෂ පරම්පරාව මේ හෙරොයින් කාරයන්ගෙන් වෙන් කරන්නත් අපි සටන් කරනවා.”
මෙම වචන කිහිපය ඔහුගේ ඝාතනයට හේතු වන්නට ඇතැයි මම විශ්වාස කරමි. මේ තමයි මගේ තාත්තා ප්‍රසිද්ධියේ කියපු අන්තිම වචන ටික.
ජනාධිපතිතුමනි, ඝාතනයට ලක් වූ මිනිසෙකුගේ මේ වදන් කෙතරම් අනාවැකිමය දැයි අද ඔප්පු වී ඇත්දැයි ඔබම සිතා බලන්න.

දශකයකට පසුවත්, මගේ පියාගේ ම්ලේච්ඡ මරණය මා තවමත් හොල්මන් කරයි. විටින් විට වේදනාවට සහ කම්පනයට මුහුණ දීමට ප්‍රතිකාර ගැනීමට මට සිදු විය. දරුවන් තිදෙනෙකුගේ මවක් ලෙස මම සුබවාදීව ඉදිරියට යාමට දැඩි උත්සාහයක් දරමි. නමුත් අදටත් මගේ පියාගේ වියෝව ඒ සතුට බිඳ දමමින් මා තුළ දරාගත නොහැකි වේදනාවකින් පුරවයි. සමහර විට ආදරය කරන කෙනෙකුගේ හදිසි, ඛේදජනක මරණයෙන් සියලුම පවුල් දුක් විඳින්නේ එලෙස විය හැකිය. ප්‍රගීත් එක්නැලිගොඩ, ලසන්ත වික්‍රමතුංග සහ වසීම් තාජුඩීන්ගේ පවුල් - ඔවුන් සියලු දෙනාම තම ජීවිතයේ සෑම දිනකම මේ කම්පනයෙන් ජීවත් වෙනවා විය යුතුය. අපේ ජීවිතය කෙතරම් පිරිපුන් වුවත්, අපට කුමන ආශිර්වාදයක් ලැබුණත්, කිසිදා පිරවිය නොහැකි හිස්බවක් අපගේ හදවත් තුළ තිබේ.
මෙම වින්දිතයින්ට සහ ඔවුන්ගේ පවුල්වලට කිසිදා යුක්තිය ඉටු වී නැත. මේ මිනීමරුවන් උසාවියට ​​ගෙනැවිත් ඔවුන්ගේ අපරාධවලට මුහුණ දීමට ඔවුන්ට අයිතියක් ඇත.
ඔබ මේ ‘එකම රටක් එක නීතියක්’ යටතේ ප්‍රචාරක කටයුතු කර ජනාධිපතිවරණය ජයග්‍රහණය කළා. අද ඒ වදන් මැතිවරණයක් ජයග්‍රහණය කිරීම සඳහා කරන සටන් පාඨවලට වඩා වැඩි දෙයක් බවට පත්වෙලා.

දැන්, මරණ දණ්ඩනයට නියම වී සිටින සෑම සිරකරුවෙක්ම ජනාධිපති සමාව ඉල්ලා සිටියහොත්, ඔබට එය ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළ නොහැක. වැරදිකරුවන් වූ සිරකරුවන් ජනාධිපති සමාව මගින් අත්තනෝමතික ලෙස නිදහස් කරන්නේ නම්, බන්ධනාගාර කුමකටද? අපිට නීති මොකටද? ඇයි අපිට විනිශ්චයකාරවරු ඉන්නේ?

ඔබේ නිවට තීරණ නිසා අද මේ රට අවනීතියයි. මෙය වහල් ජාතියකි, ස්වාමියෙකුගේ ග්‍රහණයේ සිටින ඔවුන්ව නියත මරණය කරා ගෙන යයි. අද මේ රටේ නියම පාලකයා ඔබ නොවේ. ඔබ එය පිළිගත යුතුයි. ඔබ වෙනත් බලවේග විසින් හසුරුවන රූකඩයක් පමණයි.

මහතීර් මොහොමඩ්, ලී ක්වාන් යූ හෝ ව්ලැඩිමීර් පුටින් වැනි නායකයෙකු ලෙස ඔබ දැක් වූ දැක්ම කෙරෙහි විශ්වාසය තබමින් මිලියන 6.9ක ශ්‍රී ලාංකික පුරවැසියන් ඔබ කෙරෙහි විශ්වාසය තබමින් ඔබව ජනාධිපති ධූරයට ගෙන ගියහ. ඔවුන් ඔබට පාර්ලිමේන්තුවේ තුනෙන් දෙකක බලයක් ලබා දුන්නා. 20 වැනි ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධන පනත් කෙටුම්පත ගෙන ඒමට ඔවුන් ඔබට අවස්ථාව ලබා දුන්නා.

සියලුම ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන්ට ප්‍රයෝජනවත් වන දුෂ්කර තීරණ ගැනීමට ඔබට අවශ්‍ය තවත් මෙවලම් මොනවාද? ඒ වෙනුවට, ඔබ රූකඩයක් වීමට තෝරාගෙන ඇත. ඔබේ පාලනයෙන් යහපත් රටක් කවදා හෝ උදාවේ යැයි දැන් සිතිය නොහැක. පසුගිය වසරේ ඔබගේ ක්‍රියාවන් එය මනාව පෙන්නුම් කර ඇත.

මගේ තාත්තා දැන් ඉතිහාසයේ පාදසටහනක්. නමුත් ඔහු තම මුණුබුරන්ට ආඩම්බර වීමට සහ මතක තබා ගැනීමට බොහෝ දේ ඉතිරි කර ඇත. ඔබ ඉතිරි කිරීමට බලාපොරොත්තු වන උරුමය කුමක්ද? ඔබ නැති වූ විට ඔබේ අලුත උපන් මිනිබිරියට ආඩම්බරයෙන් කතා කිරීමට ඔබ ඉතිරි කරන්නේ කුමක්ද?

මම ජිවත්වන බෞද්ධ ශික්ෂාපදයට අනුව දුමින්ද සිල්වා හිටපු මන්ත්‍රීවරයාට මම දැනටමත් සමාව දී අවසන්. එහෙත් පාලකයන් සාධාරණ විය යුතු බවත් රටේ නීතිය ආරක්ෂා කළ යුතු බවත් බුදුදහම උගන්වයි. ඔබගේ ක්‍රියාවන් නීතියේ ආධිපත්‍යය පළුදු කර ඇති අතර යුක්තිය සමච්චල් කර ඇත.

මහාධිකරණ විනිසුරුවරුන් තිදෙනෙකුගෙන් දෙදෙනෙකු දුමින්ද සිල්වා මගේ පියා ඝාතනය කළ බවට තීන්දු කර මිනීමරුවන්ට මරණ දඬුවම නියම කළා. විත්තිකරුවන් ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණයට අභියාචනයක් ඉදිරිපත් කළා. එවකට අගවිනිසුරුවරයා ඇතුළු ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණයේ විනිසුරුවරුන් පස් දෙනෙකු මහාධිකරණ තීන්දුව තහවුරු කළා. මගේ පියා ඝාතනය කළ නීති විරෝධී රැස්වීමේ නායකයා දුමින්ද සිල්වා බව ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණයේ විනිසුරුවරු පස් දෙනෙක් තීරණය කළා. මහාධිකරණ සහ ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණ විනිසුරුවරුන්ගේ තීන්දු කුණු කූඩයට දමා අවසන් තීන්දු තීරණ ගන්නේ ඔබ නම් මේ රටේ නීතිවලින් ඇති ප්‍රයෝජනය කුමක්ද?

මිලියන 6.9 ක ජනතාවක් ඔබ කෙරෙහි විශ්වාසය තබා මේ රටේ නායකයා ලෙස ඔබ තෝරා ගත්තා. ඒවාට පිළිතුරු දීමට ඔබ දැන් සූදානම් විය යුතුය. ඔබගේ වර්තමාන හැසිරීම නැවත කිසි දිනෙක රාජපක්ෂ කෙනෙකුට රට පාලනය කිරීමට ඉඩ නොතබන බවට සහතිකයි.

දැන් ඔබ දුමින්ද සිල්වා නිදහස් කිරීමට විනිසුරුවරුන් හත් දෙනකු දුන් තීන්දුව ඉවත දැමූ ඔබ ඔහුව මහ ඇමැතිවරයකු ලෙස නම් කර නැවත දේශපාලනයට ගෙන ඒමට කටයුතු කළොත් මම පුදුම නොවන්නෙමි. මේ රට දැන් වහලුන්ගේ ජාතියක් බවට පරිවර්තනය වී ඇති අතර, ඔවුන්ගේ ස්වාමිවරුන් විසින් ඔවුන්ගේම විනාශය කරා ගෙන යමින් සිටිති. නමුත් ජනතාව සදාකාලික අසාධාරණය ඉවසන්නේ නැහැ.
ජනාධිපතිතුමනි, ඔබේ පාලනය අසාධාරණය බෝ කරයි, ජනතාව මේ විලංගු කඩා නැගිටින දවස වැඩි ඈතක නොවේ. එය පැමිණි විට ඔබ වැනි කොන්ද පණ නැති යුක්තියට හා නීතියට ගරු නොකරන මිනිසුන්ට දේශපාලන අනාගතයක් අහිමි වනු ඇත.
මෙය 2022 ජූනි 8 වැනි දින “ඩේලි එෆ්ටී” හි පළවන “දේශපාලන ස්පන්දනය” තීරුව සඳහා ලියන ලද ලිපියක යාවත්කාලීන අනුවාදයකි. එයට මෙතැනින් ප්‍රවේශ විය හැක:

ta.title: துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு ஜனாதிபதி மன்னிப்பு வழங்கியதன் பின்னர் கோட்டாபயவுக்கு ஹிருணிகா எழுதிய கடிதம்
body_lines: வரலாற்றுச் சிறப்புமிக்க தீர்ப்பாக வர்ணிக்கப்படும் வகையில், துமிந்த சில்வா என அழைக்கப்படும் அருமதுர லோரன்ஸ் ரொமெலோ துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ வழங்கிய ஜனாதிபதி மன்னிப்பின் செயற்பாட்டை இடைநிறுத்தி இலங்கை உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் 2022 மே 31 அன்று இடைக்கால உத்தரவைப் பிறப்பித்தது. முன்னாள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினரும், முன்னாள் மாகாண சபை உறுப்பினரும் கொலைக் குற்றச்சாட்டில் குற்றவாளியாக அறிவிக்கப்பட்டு மரண தண்டனை விதிக்கப்பட்டிருந்தார். ஜூன் 2021 இல் மன்னிக்கப்பட்ட பின்னர், துமிந்த சில்வா ஜூலை மாதம் ஜனாதிபதி ராஜபக்ஷவினால் தேசிய வீடமைப்பு அபிவிருத்தி அதிகார சபையின் தலைவராக நியமிக்கப்பட்டார். இதற்கு முன்னர் ஜனாதிபதி மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவின் காலத்தில், துமிந்த சில்வா பாதுகாப்பு அமைச்சின் கண்காணிப்பு பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினராகவும், கோட்டாபய பாதுகாப்பு அமைச்சின் செயலாளராகவும் பணியாற்றினார்.

துமிந்த சில்வா, 2011 ஆம் ஆண்டு ஒக்டோபர் 8 ஆம் திகதி, உள்ளூராட்சி சபைத் தேர்தலின் போது, ​​துப்பாக்கிச் சூட்டுச் சம்பவத்தில் ஈடுபட்டார். அங்கு முன்னாள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர "லக்கி ஐயா" மற்றும் அவரது பாதுகாவலர்கள் மூவர் கொல்லப்பட்டனர். துமிந்த சில்வா மற்றும் நால்வருக்கு இலங்கை உயர்நீதிமன்றம் தண்டனை விதித்தது. அவரது கூட்டாளிகள் கொலைக் குற்றத்திற்காக செப்டம்பர் 16, 2016 அன்று அவர்களுக்கு மரண தண்டனை விதித்தனர். அதைத் தொடர்ந்து தண்டனை மேல்முறையீடு செய்யப்பட்டது, ஆனால் உச்ச நீதிமன்றத்தின் ஐந்து நீதிபதிகள் கொண்ட பெஞ்ச் அக்டோபர் 16, 2018 அன்று ஒருமனதாக 51 பக்கத் தீர்ப்பில் சில்வாவின் கொலைக் குற்றத்தை உறுதி செய்தது. 2016 ஆம் ஆண்டு உயர் நீதிமன்றத்தின் தண்டனையை 2018 இல் உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் உறுதி செய்த போதிலும், துமிந்த சில்வா ஜூன் 2021 இல் சுதந்திர மனிதராக இருந்தார்.

துமிந்த சில்வா பொசன் போயா தினத்தன்று ஜனாதிபதியின் மன்னிப்பைப் பெற்ற அதிர்ஷ்டசாலி. ஜனாதிபதி நந்தசேன கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ, அரசியலமைப்பின் 34(1) வது சரத்தை பிரயோகித்து, 2021 ஜூன் 24 பொசன் போயா தினத்தன்று தண்டனை பெற்ற 93 கைதிகளுக்கு ஜனாதிபதி மன்னிப்பு வழங்கினார். இவர்களில் 16 தமிழ் அரசியல் கைதிகள் கொடூரமான பயங்கரவாதத் தடைச் சட்டத்தின் (PTA) கீழ் குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்டு தண்டனை பெற்றவர்கள். ) மற்ற 77 கைதிகளில், 76 பேர் ஒப்பீட்டளவில் சிறிய குற்றங்களுக்காக தண்டனை அனுபவித்தவர்கள். இந்த மன்னிப்புகள் மற்றும் வெளியீடுகள் (ஒன்றைத் தவிர) எந்த சர்ச்சையையும் உருவாக்கவில்லை. எனினும் கொலைக் குற்றவாளி துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்கப்பட்டு மரண தண்டனையிலிருந்து விடுவிக்கப்பட்டது பெரும் சர்ச்சையை ஏற்படுத்தியது.

முன்னாள் நீதியமைச்சர் தலதா அத்துகோரள முதல் அமெரிக்கத் தூதுவர் அலைனா வரை பல்வேறு தரப்பிலிருந்தும் செங்கல்பட்டு பலர் இருந்தனர். பி. டெப்லிட்ஸ். இலங்கை சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம் (BASL) ஒரு விரிவான அறிக்கையில் குற்றவாளியும் முன்னாள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினருமான துமிந்த சில்வாவிற்கு ஜனாதிபதியின் மன்னிப்பைக் கண்டித்ததுடன், அது "நியாயமற்றது மற்றும் தன்னிச்சையானது" என்று கூறியது.

இலங்கை சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம் (BASL) ஜனாதிபதி ராஜபக்ஷவுக்கு ஒரு கடிதம் எழுதியது, அது குறிப்பிட்ட பிரச்சினைகள் குறித்து விளக்கம் கேட்டு ஊடகங்களுக்கு அறிக்கையை வெளியிட்டது. BASL எழுப்பிய பிரச்சினைகளில் அடங்கும்
(அ) ​​அரசியலமைப்பின் 34 (1) பிரிவின் கீழ் மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதற்காக துமிந்த சில்வா தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்டதன் அடிப்படையில்;
(ஆ) அத்தகைய மன்னிப்பை வழங்குவதில் கவனத்தில் கொள்ளப்பட்ட சூழ்நிலைகள்;
(இ) துமிந்த சில்வாவின் வழக்கு தற்போது தண்டனை விதிக்கப்பட்டுள்ள மற்றவர்களை விட ஏன் தனித்து நிற்கிறது என்பதற்கான காரணங்கள்;

(ஈ) துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதற்கு முன்னர் 34 (1) வது சரத்தின் விதியின்படி அதிமேதகு ஜனாதிபதியால் விசாரணை நீதிபதிகளிடமிருந்து அறிக்கை கோரப்பட்டதா மற்றும் அவ்வாறாயின் அந்த அறிக்கையின் உள்ளடக்கங்கள்;
(இ) துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதற்கு முன்னர் சட்டமா அதிபரின் ஆலோசனை கோரப்பட்டதா மற்றும் அவ்வாறாயின் அத்தகைய ஆலோசனையின் உள்ளடக்கங்கள் யாவை;
(ஊ) துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதற்கு முன்னர் நீதி அமைச்சரின் பரிந்துரை பெறப்பட்டதா மற்றும் அவ்வாறாயின் நீதி அமைச்சர் அத்தகைய பரிந்துரையை செய்தாரா;

"மேலே கூறப்பட்ட ஏதேனும் ஒன்று அல்லது அதற்கு மேற்பட்ட கருத்தாய்வுகள் தற்போதைய வழக்கில் திருப்தியடையவில்லை என்றால், துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு வழங்கப்பட்ட மன்னிப்பு நியாயமற்றது மற்றும் தன்னிச்சையானது மற்றும் சட்டத்தின் ஆட்சிக்கு சிதைவை ஏற்படுத்தும் என்று இலங்கை சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம் கூறுகிறது. நீதி நிர்வாகம் தொடர்பாக பொதுமக்களின் நம்பிக்கையை இழந்துள்ளது” என்று ஜனாதிபதி ராஜபக்ஷவுக்கு BASL கடிதத்தில் குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ளது.

துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு ஜனாதிபதி மன்னிப்பு வழங்கியதையடுத்து, பாதிக்கப்பட்ட பாரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திரவின் மனைவி சுமனா பிரேமச்சந்திர, மகளும், முன்னாள் நாடாளுமன்ற உறுப்பினருமான ஹிருணிகா பிரேமச்சந்திர, இலங்கை மனித உரிமைகள் ஆணைக்குழுவின் முன்னாள் ஆணையாளர் கசாலி ஹுசைன் பி.சி.யுடன் இணைந்து அடிப்படை உரிமை மீறல் மனுக்களை தாக்கல் செய்தனர். துமிந்த சில்வாவின் ஜனாதிபதி மன்னிப்பு சட்டத்தில் செல்லாது. அரசியலமைப்பின் 10, 11, 12 மற்றும் 12(1) ஆகிய பிரிவுகளின் கீழ் உத்தரவாதம் அளிக்கப்பட்டுள்ள தங்களின் அடிப்படை உரிமைகள் மீறப்பட்டுள்ளதாக மனுதாரர்கள் நீதிமன்றத்தில் அறிவிக்க வேண்டும் என்று கோரினர்.

பூர்வாங்க விசாரணைகளின் பின்னர், நீதியரசர் ப்ரீத்தி பத்மன் சூரசேன, நீதியரசர் யசந்த கோதாகொட மற்றும் நீதியரசர் அச்சல வெங்கப்புலி ஆகிய மூன்று நீதிபதிகள் அடங்கிய உச்ச நீதிமன்ற அமர்வு, ஜனாதிபதியின் பொது மன்னிப்பின் செயல்பாட்டை இடைநிறுத்தி இடைக்கால உத்தரவைப் பிறப்பித்தது. அரசியலமைப்பின் விதி 12(1) இன் விதிமுறைகள் மற்றும் மனுக்கள் செப்டம்பர் 1, 2022 அன்று வாதத்திற்கு எடுத்துக்கொள்ளப்பட வேண்டும். ஜனாதிபதியின் மன்னிப்பை இடைநிறுத்துவதற்கான இடைக்கால உத்தரவு மூன்று FR மனுக்களின் இறுதித் தீர்மானம் வரை அமலில் இருக்கும்.

இடைக்கால உத்தரவுக்கு அமைய, துமிந்த சில்வாவை உடனடியாக அமுலுக்கு வரும் வகையில் சிறைச்சாலைகள் ஆணையாளர் நாயகத்தின் காவலில் வைக்குமாறு உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் சிஐடிக்கு உத்தரவிட்டது. இந்த இடைக்கால உத்தரவை அமுல்படுத்துவதற்கு சிஐடிக்கு உதவுமாறு சட்டமா அதிபருக்கு உத்தரவிடப்பட்டது. துமிந்த சில்வாவின் கடவுச்சீட்டை முடக்கி உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் இடைக்கால உத்தரவையும் பிறப்பித்துள்ளது.

சட்டத்தரணி - ஜெனரல் சஞ்சய் ராஜரத்தினம் பொலிஸ் மா அதிபருக்கு (IGP) கடிதம் எழுதி, துமிந்த சில்வாவை நாட்டை விட்டு வெளியேறுவதைத் தடுக்கும் உத்தரவு மற்றும் அவரது கடவுச்சீட்டைப் பறிமுதல் செய்வதும் அடங்கிய SC இன் இடைக்கால உத்தரவுகள் குறித்து அவருக்குத் தெரியப்படுத்தியது. குற்றப் புலனாய்வுத் திணைக்களத்தின் பணிப்பாளர் ஊடாக.

இதேவேளை, தேசிய வீடமைப்பு அபிவிருத்தி அதிகார சபையின் தலைவர் துமிந்த சில்வா, மருத்துவ சிகிச்சைக்காக ஸ்ரீ ஜயவர்தனபுர வைத்தியசாலையில் அனுமதிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளார். SC இடைக்கால உத்தரவின் விளைவாக, CID அவரை மருத்துவமனையின் நரம்பியல் பிரிவில் சில்வாவைக் கைது செய்தது. அவர் சிறப்பு காவலில் வைக்கப்பட்டார். இதனையடுத்து துமிந்த சில்வா சிறைச்சாலைக்கு கொண்டு செல்லப்பட்டார்.

சுமண பிரேமச்சந்திரவுக்காக சட்டத்தரணி திமுத்து குருப்புஆராச்சியின் அறிவுறுத்தலின் பேரில் தமிந்த விஜேரத்ன மற்றும் சுந்தரமூர்த்தி ஜனகன் ஆகியோருடன் சட்டத்தரணி எராஜ் டி சில்வா ஆஜராகி வாதாடினர். ஹிருணிகா பிரேமச்சந்திரவுக்காக எம்.ஏ.சுமந்திரன், ஜெப்ரி அழகரட்ணம் பி.சி.கசாலி ஹுசைன் ஆகியோர் ஆஜராகினர்.

அட்டர்னி ஜெனரல் சார்பில் கூடுதல் சொலிசிட்டர் ஜெனரல் நெரின் புல்லே ஆஜரானார். துமிந்த சில்வா சார்பில் ஜனாதிபதி சட்டத்தரணி காமினி மாரப்பன மற்றும் நவீன் மாரப்பன பி.சி. கே. கனக ஈஸ்வரன் பி.சி.யால் அறிவுறுத்தப்பட்ட மூத்த வழக்கறிஞர் ஜி.ஜி. மனுக்களின் பிரதிவாதியான இலங்கை சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம் சார்பில் அருள்பிரகாசம் ஆஜராகியிருந்தார்.

உச்ச நீதிமன்ற இடைக்கால உத்தரவு ஒரு முக்கிய தீர்ப்பாக பரவலாகப் பாராட்டப்பட்டது. ஒரு இடைக்கால உத்தரவு என்றாலும், உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் தீர்ப்பின் மூலம், மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதில் நிறைவேற்று ஜனாதிபதியின் அரசியலமைப்பு சிறப்புரிமையை கேள்விக்குள்ளாக்கியுள்ளது. மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதில் நீதிமன்றத்திற்கு ஆதிக்கம் இல்லை என்ற ஏஜியின் நிலைப்பாடு, வழக்கப்படி நீதித்துறை கட்டுப்பாட்டை நடைமுறைப்படுத்தியதாகத் தெரியவில்லை. அந்த வகையில், உச்ச நீதிமன்றத்தின் தீர்ப்பு உண்மையிலேயே வரலாற்றுச் சிறப்பு மிக்கதாகும். எவ்வாறாயினும், இது தற்போதைக்கு ஒரு இடைக்கால உத்தரவு மட்டுமே மற்றும் எதிர்காலத்தில் என்ன நடக்கும் - மேலும் விசாரணைகள் தொடரும் - பார்க்க வேண்டும்.

துமிந்த சில்வாவின் ஜனாதிபதியின் பொது மன்னிப்பு இடைநிறுத்தப்பட்டு மீண்டும் சிறையில் அடைக்கப்பட்ட இடைக்கால உத்தரவு, பாதிக்கப்பட்ட பாரத லக்ஷ்மனின் துணிச்சலான மகள் ஹிருணிகா பிரேமச்சந்திர மீது மீண்டும் ஊடகங்களின் கவனத்தைத் திருப்பியுள்ளது. நீதிக்காகவும், அநீதிக்கு எதிராகவும் தனது அடாவடியான பிரச்சாரத்தின் மூலம் பலரது அனுதாபத்தையும் பாராட்டையும் பெற்றுள்ளார் முன்னாள் எம்.பி. “கோதா வீட்டுக்குப் போ” போராட்டம் அண்மைக் காலமாகப் பரவி வந்தாலும், அதையெல்லாம் ஆரம்பித்து வைத்தது ஹிருணிகாவும், துணிச்சலான பெண் தோழர்களும் என்பதை நினைவில் கொள்ள வேண்டும். மிரிஹானவில் உள்ள ஜனாதிபதியின் தனிப்பட்ட இல்லம்.

ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ கடந்த ஆண்டு பொசன் போயா தினத்தன்று தனது தந்தையின் கொலையாளிக்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்கியபோது, ​​ஹிருணிகா பிரேமச்சந்திர, நிறைவேற்று ஜனாதிபதிக்கு சிங்கள மொழியில் உணர்ச்சிகரமான கடிதம் ஒன்றை எழுதினார். “Colombo Telegraph” இணையத்தளம் ஆங்கில மொழியாக்கத்தை வெளியிட்டது. அவரது தந்தை கொல்லப்படுவதற்கு முன்னும் பின்னும் என்ன நடந்தது என்பதன் சாராம்சத்தை நுண்ணறிவுமிக்க கடிதம் உள்ளடக்கியது. மிக முக்கியமாக, தற்போதைய ராஜபக்ச எதிர்ப்புக் கிளர்ச்சியின் பின்னணியில் பார்க்கும்போது இது ஓரளவு தீர்க்கதரிசனமானது. அந்தக் கடிதத்தை முழுவதுமாக மீண்டும் தயாரித்து இக்கட்டுரையை முடிக்கிறேன் –
இலங்கை ஜனநாயக சோசலிச குடியரசின் தலைவர் ஜனாதிபதி செயலகம்
Re: பாரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திரவை கொலை செய்த குற்றச்சாட்டின் பேரில் ஏழு பேரால் தண்டிக்கப்பட்ட ஆர்.துமுண்ட சில்வாவுக்கு ஜனாதிபதி பொது மன்னிப்பு வழங்குதல்.
நான் உங்கள் சகோதரரும், முன்னாள் ஜனாதிபதி மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவின் தொழிற்சங்க ஆலோசகரும், முன்னாள் நாடாளுமன்ற உறுப்பினருமான பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திரவின் மகள்.

அக்டோபர் 8, 2011 அன்று, எனது அன்புக்குரிய தந்தை பட்டப்பகலில் சுட்டுக் கொல்லப்பட்டார், அவரது உடலில் 20 க்கும் மேற்பட்ட தோட்டாக்கள் துளைக்கப்பட்டன. முன்னாள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் ஆர் துமிந்த சில்வாவின் கொலைக்கு இரண்டு உயர் நீதிமன்ற நீதிபதிகள் தண்டனை வழங்கினர், மேலும் அவர்களின் தீர்ப்பை பிரதம நீதியரசர் உட்பட உச்ச நீதிமன்றத்தின் ஐந்து நீதிபதிகள் உறுதி செய்தனர். இன்று அவருக்கு ஜனாதிபதி மன்னிப்பு வழங்கியிருக்கிறீர்கள்.

அந்த துரதிஷ்டமான நாளில், எனக்கும் என் அம்மாவுக்கும் ஒரு அநாமதேய தொலைபேசி அழைப்பு வந்தது, என் அப்பா சுடப்பட்டதாக எங்களுக்குத் தெரிவித்தார். இதுபோன்ற தொலைபேசி அழைப்புகளை நாங்கள் பயன்படுத்தியதால், ஆரம்பத்தில் நாங்கள் பீதி அடையவில்லை. கடந்த காலங்களில் எனது தந்தை பாதிப்பில் இருந்து பாதுகாப்பாக இருக்கிறார் என்பதை உறுதிப்படுத்திக் கொண்டு அவர்கள் எப்போதும் பின்தொடர்ந்தனர்.

1999 ஆம் ஆண்டு பொரளையில் ஜனாதிபதி சந்திரிக்கா பண்டாரநாயக்க குமாரதுங்கவிற்கான இறுதி தேர்தல் பேரணியை ஏற்பாடு செய்தவர் எனது தந்தை. அந்த பேரணியின் மீது விடுதலைப் புலிகளின் குண்டுத் தாக்குதலுக்குப் பிறகு, எனது தந்தை எங்கள் குடும்பத்தினரை தொலைபேசியில் தொடர்பு கொண்டு, குண்டுவெடிப்பில் அவர் காயமடையவில்லை என்பதை உறுதிப்படுத்தினார். தினமும் காலையில் நானும் என் தம்பியும் பள்ளிக்கு செல்லும் முன் அப்பாவை வணங்குவோம். பேரணியில் வெடிகுண்டு வெடித்த மறுநாள் காலை அவருக்கு அஞ்சலி செலுத்த நாங்கள் நடந்து சென்றபோது, ​​அவருடைய அங்கியில் இன்னும் இரத்தம் தோய்ந்திருந்தது எனக்கு தெளிவாக நினைவிருக்கிறது.

போரினால் பாதிக்கப்பட்ட நாட்டில், 30 ஆண்டுகால அவரது அரசியல் வாழ்க்கையில் இந்த சம்பவங்கள் சகஜம். ஆனால் அக்டோபர் 2011 அன்று, தொலைபேசி ஒலிப்பதை நிறுத்தவே இல்லை. நானும் அம்மாவும் கொழும்பில் உள்ள தேசிய மருத்துவமனைக்கு புறப்பட முடிவு செய்தோம். நாங்கள் அங்கு சென்றதும், பிணவறைக்கு செல்லும்படி கூறினோம்.

பத்து வருடங்கள் கடந்துவிட்டன, என் தந்தையின் சோக மரணத்தின் நினைவு இன்னும் என்னை நசுக்குகிறது, என்னை அச்சத்தால் நிரப்புகிறது மற்றும் முழு உலகமும் என்னைச் சுற்றி இடிந்து விழுவதைப் போன்ற உணர்வை ஏற்படுத்துகிறது.

என் தந்தை படுகொலை செய்யப்பட்டபோது எனக்கு 23 வயது. அவர் வாழ்நாளில் நீதிமன்ற அறையிலோ, காவல் நிலையத்திலோ காலடி எடுத்து வைக்காத நானும் என் தாயும் ஐந்து வருடங்களாக என் தந்தைக்கு நீதி கேட்டு நீதிமன்ற அறையிலிருந்து நீதிமன்ற அறைக்கு நடைபயணம் மேற்கொண்டோம்.

எந்தவொரு இளம் பெண்ணின் வாழ்க்கையிலும் 20 கள், மகிழ்ச்சி மற்றும் விளையாட்டுத்தனத்திற்கான நேரம். நான் அந்த ஆண்டுகளை சக்திவாய்ந்த, கொடூரமான கொலைகாரர்களின் குழுவுடன் கொம்புகளைப் பூட்டிக் கொண்டிருந்தேன். அவர் இறந்தபோது, ​​பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திரவுக்கு ஒரு மகள் இருந்தாள் என்பது யாருக்கும் தெரியாது. அவரது மறைவுக்குப் பிறகு, நாடு முழுவதும் என் பெயர் ஒரே இரவில் தெரிந்தது. நான் பயங்கரமான எதிரிகளை உருவாக்கினேன். ஏனென்றால் என் தந்தையைக் கொன்றவர்களுக்கு எதிராக நீதிமன்றத்தில் நீதியைத் தொடர முடிவு செய்தேன்.

ராஜபக்ச ஆட்சியில் இருந்த போது துமிந்த சில்வா ஒரு நொடி கூட சிறையில் இருந்ததில்லை. எனது தந்தையின் கொலையாளிகளையும் பட்டப்பகலில் மக்களைக் கொன்று குவித்ததையும் உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் கூறியது இவர்தான். குற்றப் புலனாய்வுப் பிரிவின் பணிப்பாளர் ஷானி அபேசேகரவும் அவரது அதிகாரிகளும் தான் எனது தந்தையின் கொலை தொடர்பில் ஒவ்வொரு சாட்சியங்களையும் ஒழுங்காகவும் குற்றஞ்சாட்ட முடியாத வகையிலும் சேகரித்து நுணுக்கமான விசாரணைகளை மேற்கொண்டனர். இன்று, ஷானி அபேசேகர ஒரு பொலிஸ் உத்தியோகத்தராக தனது கடமைகளை சுதந்திரமாக நிறைவேற்றும் துணிச்சலுக்காக தவிக்கிறார்.
எனது தந்தை கொல்லப்பட்டது முதல் அவருக்கு இறுதி அஞ்சலி செலுத்த கட்சி வேறுபாடுகளை மீறி அரசியல்வாதிகள் குவிந்தனர். ஆனால் நீங்கள் வரவே இல்லை.

மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷ, சமல் ராஜபக்ஷ, பசில் ராஜபக்ஷ, ஷிரந்தி ராஜபக்ஷ, நாமல் ராஜபக்ஷ, யோஷித ராஜபக்ஷ மற்றும் ரோஹித ராஜபக்ஷ உங்கள் குடும்பத்தினர் அனைவரும் எனது தந்தையின் இறுதி ஊர்வலத்தில் கலந்து கொண்டனர். ஆனால் நீங்கள் இன்னும் தோன்றவில்லை. நீங்கள் ஸ்ரீ ஜயவர்தனபுர வைத்தியசாலையில் துமிந்த சில்வாவின் படுக்கையில் இருந்தீர்கள். துமிந்த சில்வாவை சிங்கப்பூருக்கு அழைத்துச் செல்ல ஹெலிகாப்டரை ஆர்டர் செய்வதே உங்கள் முன்னுரிமை.

பாரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர 1977 இல் மறைந்த டி.பி.யின் வழிகாட்டுதலின் கீழ் அரசியலில் பிரவேசித்தார். இளங்கரத்ன. அதனைத் தொடர்ந்து, இலங்கை மகாஜன பக்ஷயவின் ஸ்தாபக உறுப்பினராக விஜய குமாரதுங்கவுடன் இணைந்து அரசியல் நடவடிக்கைகளில் ஈடுபட்டார். 1994 இல் ஸ்ரீலங்கா மகாஜன பக்ஷயவை பிரதிநிதித்துவப்படுத்தி பாராளுமன்றத்தில் நுழைந்த அவர், 1996 இல் ஜனாதிபதி சந்திரிக்கா பண்டாரநாயக்க குமாரதுங்கவின் கீழ் ஸ்ரீலங்கா சுதந்திரக் கட்சியின் உறுப்பினரானார். நீல கொடி. சில சமயங்களில், கொழும்பு மாவட்டத்தில் ஸ்ரீலங்கா சுதந்திரக் கட்சிக்காக தனித்து நின்று போராடினார்.

அவர்கள் சந்தித்த நாளிலிருந்தே எனது தந்தை உங்கள் சகோதரர் மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவுடன் நெருங்கிய நட்புறவை வளர்த்தார். இதனாலேயே எனது தந்தை அவரை “மஹிந்த அய்யா” என்றே குறிப்பிடுவார். ஒவ்வொரு அடியிலும் மகிழ்ச்சியிலும் துக்கத்திலும் எனது தந்தை மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவுடன் நின்றார்.

ஜனாதிபதி சந்திரிக்கா குமாரதுங்கவின் எதிர்க்கட்சித் தலைவர் பதவியை வேறு ஒருவருக்கு வழங்குவதற்கு எதிராகப் போராடியவர் எனது தந்தை. மகிந்த ராஜபக்சவை பிரதமராக நியமிக்க வேண்டும் என்று தலைமை முடிவு செய்திருந்த நிலையில், பாரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திரதான் பிரதமராக பதவியேற்க வேண்டும் என்று போராடினார். ஒரு காலத்தில் ஸ்ரீலங்கா சுதந்திரக் கட்சியில் அங்கம் வகித்த SLPP யில் உள்ள எந்த ஒரு அரசியல்வாதிக்கும் இந்தக் கதை தெரியும்.

அவர்களில் யாரை வேண்டுமானாலும் கேட்கலாம், அவர்கள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர்களா இல்லையா என்பதைப் பொருட்படுத்தாமல். இந்த வரலாற்றை பிரதமரிடமிருந்தே கற்றுக்கொள்ளலாம். சகோதரனாகக் கருதும் தனது நண்பர் மகிந்தவுக்கே முழுமையாக அர்ப்பணித்தவர். அவர் கொள்கையில் நிற்கிறார் என்று அவர் நம்பிய இடத்தில், என் தந்தை அசைய மாட்டார். தொழிலாளர்களுக்காகவும், அடித்தட்டு மக்களுக்காகவும் தொடர்ந்து குரல் எழுப்பினார். அவர் அரசியல்வாதியாக சொத்து குவிக்கவில்லை. மாறாக, ஆட்சியிலும் எதிர்க்கட்சியிலும் உள்ள அனைவருக்கும் நண்பராகவும், சகோதரனாகவும் செயல்பட்டார்.
ஆனால் அவர் கொல்லப்படுவதற்கு சில நாட்களுக்கு முன்பு ஒரு பொது மேடையில் ஒரு தவறு செய்தார்.

“கொலன்னாவை அமைப்பாளர், ஜனாதிபதியின் சகோதரரான பாதுகாப்புச் செயலாளரை அவர் ஹெரோயின் விற்கும் ஒவ்வொரு வீட்டிற்கும் அழைத்துச் செல்கிறார். இதுதான் என் கவலை. பிரசன்னாவின் வெற்றியை மட்டும் உறுதி செய்ய மாட்டோம் என்பதை இந்த இடத்தில் தெரிவித்துக் கொள்ள வேண்டும். நாங்கள் மிகவும் சிரமப்பட்டு கட்டியெழுப்பிய ராஜபக்ச தலைமுறையை இந்த ஹெரோயின் வியாபாரிகளிடமிருந்து துண்டிக்க போராடுவோம்.
இந்த சில வார்த்தைகள் அவரது கொலைக்கு வழிவகுத்திருக்கலாம் என்று நான் நம்புகிறேன். என் தந்தை பொதுவில் சொன்ன கடைசி வார்த்தைகள் இவை.
ஜனாதிபதி, கொலை செய்யப்பட்ட ஒரு மனிதனின் இந்த வார்த்தைகள் இன்று எவ்வளவு தீர்க்கதரிசனமாக நிரூபிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளன என்பதை நீங்களே கேட்டுக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.

ஒரு தசாப்தத்திற்குப் பிறகு, என் தந்தையின் கொடூரமான மரணம் இன்னும் என்னை ஆட்டிப்படைக்கிறது. வலி மற்றும் அதிர்ச்சியைச் சமாளிக்க அவ்வப்போது நான் சிகிச்சை பெற வேண்டியிருந்தது. மூன்று குழந்தைகளின் தாயாக, நான் நம்பிக்கையுடன் இருக்கவும் முன்னேறவும் கடினமாக முயற்சி செய்கிறேன். ஆனால் இன்றளவும், என் தந்தையின் இழப்பு அந்த மகிழ்ச்சியைக் குறைத்து, தாங்க முடியாத வேதனையில் என்னை நிரப்புகிறது. ஒருவேளை எல்லாக் குடும்பங்களும் இப்படித்தான் நேசிப்பவரின் திடீர், சோகமான மரணத்தை அனுபவிக்கின்றன. பிரகீத் எக்னலிகொட, லசந்த விக்ரமதுங்க மற்றும் வசீம் தாஜுதீன் ஆகியோரின் குடும்பங்கள் - அவர்கள் அனைவரும் தங்கள் வாழ்நாளின் ஒவ்வொரு நாளும் இந்த அதிர்ச்சியை அனுபவித்துக்கொண்டிருக்க வேண்டும். நம் வாழ்வு எவ்வளவு நிரம்பியிருந்தாலும், என்ன ஆசீர்வாதங்களைப் பெற்றாலும், ஒருபோதும் நிரப்பப்படாத வெற்றிடம் நம் இதயத்தில் உள்ளது.
இந்த பாதிக்கப்பட்டவர்களுக்கும் அவர்களது குடும்பங்களுக்கும் நீதி வழங்கப்படவில்லை. இந்த கொலையாளிகள் நீதிமன்றத்திற்கு கொண்டு வரப்படுவதையும் அவர்களின் குற்றங்களை எதிர்கொள்ளவும் அவர்களுக்கு உரிமை உண்டு.
இந்த ‘ஒரே நாடு, ஒரே சட்டம்’ என்பதன் கீழ் ஜனாதிபதித் தேர்தலில் பிரச்சாரம் செய்து வெற்றி பெற்றீர்கள். இன்றைக்கு அந்த வார்த்தைகள் தேர்தலில் வெற்றி பெற வேண்டும் என்ற கோஷங்களைத் தவிர வேறொன்றுமில்லை.

இப்போது, ​​மரண தண்டனையில் உள்ள ஒவ்வொரு கைதியும் ஜனாதிபதியின் மன்னிப்பைக் கோரினால், நீங்கள் மறுக்க முடியாத நிலையில் இருக்கிறீர்கள். தண்டனைக் கைதிகள் தன்னிச்சையாக ஜனாதிபதியின் பொது மன்னிப்பின் மூலம் விடுவிக்கப்பட வேண்டுமென்றால், சிறைச்சாலைகள் எதற்காக? நமக்கு ஏன் சட்டங்கள் உள்ளன? நமக்கு ஏன் நீதிபதிகள்?

உங்களின் கோழைத்தனமான முடிவுகளால், இன்று இந்த நாடு சட்டமற்றது. இது அடிமைகளின் தேசம், ஒரு எஜமானரின் பிடியில் அவர்களை மரணத்திற்கு அழைத்துச் செல்கிறது. இன்று இந்த நாட்டின் உண்மையான ஆட்சியாளர் நீங்கள் அல்ல. அதை நீங்கள் ஏற்றுக்கொள்ள வேண்டும். நீங்கள் மற்ற சக்திகளால் கையாளப்படும் ஒரு பொம்மை.

மகாதீர் மொஹமட், லீ குவான் யூ அல்லது விளாடிமிர் புடின் போன்ற ஒரு தலைவராக உங்கள் பார்வையில் நம்பிக்கை வைத்து, 6.9 மில்லியன் இலங்கைப் பிரஜைகள் உங்கள் மீது நம்பிக்கை வைத்து உங்களை ஜனாதிபதி பதவிக்கு உயர்த்தினார்கள். பாராளுமன்றத்தில் மூன்றில் இரண்டு பெரும்பான்மையை உங்களுக்கு வழங்கினர். அரசியலமைப்பின் 20 வது திருத்தத்தை நீங்கள் நிறைவேற்றுவதற்கு அவர்கள் சாத்தியமாக்கினர்.

அனைத்து இலங்கையர்களுக்கும் பயனளிக்கும் கடினமான முடிவுகளை எடுக்க உங்களுக்கு இன்னும் என்ன கருவிகள் தேவைப்படலாம். மாறாக, நீங்கள் ஒரு கைப்பாவையாக மாறுவதைத் தேர்ந்தெடுத்துள்ளீர்கள். உங்கள் ஆட்சி ஒரு சிறந்த நாட்டை உருவாக்கும் என்று இப்போது கற்பனை செய்து பார்க்க முடியாது. கடந்த வருடத்தில் உங்களின் செயல்கள் அதை நிரூபித்துள்ளன.

என் தந்தை இப்போது வரலாற்றில் ஒரு அடிக்குறிப்பு. ஆனால் அவர் தனது பேரக்குழந்தைகள் பெருமைப்படுவதற்கும் நினைவில் கொள்வதற்கும் பலவற்றை விட்டுச் சென்றுள்ளார். என்ன மரபை விட்டுச் செல்வீர்கள் என்று நம்புகிறீர்கள்? நீங்கள் மறைந்தவுடன் பிறந்த பேத்தி பெருமையாகப் பேசுவதற்கு நீங்கள் எதை விட்டுச் செல்வீர்கள்?

நான் கடைப்பிடிக்கும் பௌத்த விதிகளுக்கு இணங்க, நான் ஏற்கனவே முன்னாள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் துமிந்த சில்வாவை மன்னித்துவிட்டேன். ஆனால் பௌத்தம் ஆட்சியாளர்கள் நீதியுள்ளவர்களாகவும் நாட்டின் சட்டங்களை நிலைநிறுத்தவும் வேண்டும் என்று போதிக்கிறது. உங்கள் செயல்கள் சட்டத்தின் ஆட்சியை சீர்குலைத்து நீதியை கேலிக்கூத்தாக்கிவிட்டன.

மூன்று உயர் நீதிமன்ற நீதிபதிகளில் இருவர் துமிந்த சில்வா எனது தந்தையைக் கொலைசெய்து கொலையாளிகளுக்கு மரண தண்டனை விதித்து தீர்ப்பளித்தனர். குற்றவாளிகள் உச்ச நீதிமன்றத்தில் மேல்முறையீடு செய்தனர். அப்போதைய தலைமை நீதிபதி உட்பட 5 உச்ச நீதிமன்ற நீதிபதிகள் உயர் நீதிமன்றத்தின் தீர்ப்பை உறுதி செய்தனர். துமிந்த சில்வா எனது தந்தையைக் கொன்ற சட்டவிரோதக் கூட்டத்தின் தலைவன் என்று உச்ச நீதிமன்றத்தின் ஐந்து நீதிபதிகள் தீர்மானித்துள்ளனர். உயர்நீதிமன்றம் மற்றும் உச்ச நீதிமன்ற நீதிபதிகளின் தீர்ப்புகள் குப்பையில் போடப்பட்டு, இறுதி முடிவுகளை நீங்கள் எடுக்கிறீர்கள் என்றால், இந்த நாட்டில் சட்டங்களால் என்ன பயன்?

6.9 மில்லியன் மக்கள் உங்கள் மீது நம்பிக்கை வைத்து இந்த நாட்டின் தலைவராக உங்களைத் தேர்ந்தெடுத்துள்ளனர். இப்போது நீங்கள் அவர்களுக்கு பதிலளிக்க தயாராக இருக்க வேண்டும். உங்கள் தற்போதைய நடத்தை மீண்டும் ஒரு ராஜபக்ச நாட்டை ஆள மக்கள் அனுமதிக்க மாட்டார்கள் என்பதை உறுதி செய்கிறது.

துமிந்த சில்வாவை விடுதலை செய்ய ஏழு நீதிபதிகள் வழங்கிய தீர்ப்பை நிராகரித்துள்ள நீங்கள் இப்போது அவரை முதலமைச்சராக நியமித்து மீண்டும் அரசியலுக்குக் கொண்டுவந்தாலும் ஆச்சரியப்படுவதற்கில்லை. இந்த நாடு இப்போது அடிமைகளின் தேசமாக மாற்றப்பட்டு, அவர்களின் எஜமானர்களால் தங்கள் அழிவை நோக்கித் தள்ளப்படுகிறது. ஆனால் அநீதியை மக்கள் என்றென்றும் பொறுத்துக்கொள்ள மாட்டார்கள்.

உங்கள் ஆட்சி அநீதியை வளர்க்கிறது, ஜனாதிபதி, மக்கள் இந்த கட்டுகளை உடைத்து எழும் நாள் வெகு தொலைவில் இல்லை. அது வரும்போது, ​​முதுகெலும்பு இல்லாத, நீதி மற்றும் சட்டத்தின் மீது மரியாதை இல்லாத உங்களைப் போன்றவர்களுக்கு அரசியல் எதிர்காலம் இல்லாமல் போய்விடும்.
இது ஜூன் 8, 2022 இன் "டெய்லி எஃப்டி"யில் வரும் "அரசியல் பல்ஸ்" நெடுவரிசைக்காக எழுதப்பட்ட கட்டுரையின் புதுப்பிக்கப்பட்ட பதிப்பாகும். இதை இங்கே அணுகலாம்:

en.title: Hirunika’s Letter to Gotabaya After the Presidential Pardon of Duminda Silva
body_lines: In what has been described as a historic  ruling, the Sri Lankan Supreme Court  on May 31st 2022, issued an Interim Order suspending the operation of the Presidential Pardon granted by  President  Gotabaya Rajapaksa  to Arumadura Lawrence Romelo Duminda Silva known as Duminda Silva. The former parliamentarian  and ex- provincial councillor had been convicted  on a charge of murder and sentenced to death. After  being pardoned in June 2021 , Duminda Silva  was appointed in July  as Chairman of the National Housing Development Authority by President Rajapaksa. Earlier  during the time of  President  Mahinda  Rajapaksa, Duminda  Silva served as Monitoring MP of the Defence Ministry while Gotabaya was  the Defence  Ministry Secretary.

Duminda Silva  was involved in a shooting incident on October  8 2011, during Local Authority Elections, where former MP Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra  known as “Lucky Aiya”  and three of his bodyguards were killed.The High Court of Sri Lanka convicted Duminda Silva and four of his associates for murder and imposed the death sentence on them on September 16, 2016. Subsequently the sentence was appealed but a five-Judge bench of the Supreme Court upheld the murder conviction of Silva in a unanimous 51 page verdict issued on  October 16 , 2018. Despite the High Court conviction of 2016 being upheld by the Supreme Court in 2018, Duminda Silva was a free man in  June 2021.

Duminda Silva was the fortunate recipient of a munificent Presidential pardon on Poson Poya day. President Nandasena Gotabaya Rajapaksa invoking article 34(1) of the Constitution, granted a Presidential pardon to 93 convicted prisoners on Poson Poya day of 24 June  2021. Of these 16 were Tamil political prisoners charged and convicted under the draconian Prevention of Terrorism Act (PTA). Of the 77 other prisoners, 76 were people who had been serving sentences for relatively minor offences. These pardons and releases (except for one) did not create any controversy. Murder convict Duminda Silva’s pardon and release from death row  however caused much controversy.

There were many brickbats from various quarters ranging from former Justice Minister Thalatha Athukorale to US Ambassador Alaina. B. Teplitz. The Bar Association of Sri Lanka (BASL) in a detailed statement condemned the Presidential pardon for convict and former MP Duminda Silva and stated it was “unreasonable and arbitrary”.

The Bar Association of Sri Lanka (BASL)  wrote a letter  to President Rajapaksa that sought clarification on  specified issues and released the missive to the media. Among issues raised by the  BASL  were
(a) The basis on which Duminda Silva was selected for the purpose of granting a pardon under Article 34 (1) of the Constitution;
(b) The circumstances which were taken into consideration in the granting of such pardon;
(c) The reasons as to why the case of Duminda Silva stands out from others who are currently sentenced;

(d) Whether a report was called for by His Excellency the President from the Trial Judges as required by the Proviso to Article 34 (1) prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so the contents of the report;
(e) Whether the advice of the Attorney General was called for prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so the contents of such advice;
(f) Whether the recommendation of the Minister of Justice was obtained prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so whether the Minister of Justice made such a recommendation;

“The Bar Association of Sri Lanka maintains that if any one or more considerations stated above, were not satisfied in the current case, the pardon granted to Duminda Silva would be unreasonable and arbitrary and will result in erosion to the rule of law and result in a loss of public confidence in respect of the administration of justice” stated the BASL letter to  President Rajapaksa.

Following  the presidential pardon of Duminda Silva,  the victim Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra’s wdow  Sumana Premachandra,  daughter and Former Parliamentarian Hirunika Premachandra,  along  with  former Commissioner of Human Rights Commission of Sri Lanka (HRCSL) Ghazali Hussain PC, filed  Fundamental rights petitions seeking a declaration that the presidential pardon of Duminda Silva was invalid in law. The petitioners sought a declaration  from court, that their Fundamental Rights guaranteed under Articles 10, 11, 12 and 12(1) of the Constitution have been violated.

After preliminary hearings, a  three judge bench of the Supreme Court comprising Justice Preethi Padman Surasena, Justice Yasantha Kodagoda and Justice Achala Wengappuli issued  the Interim Order suspending the operation of the Presidential Pardon  The Supreme Court granted leave to proceed with the three Fundamental Rights petitions in terms of Article 12(1) of the constitution and  fixed the petitions to be taken up for argument on September 1, 2022. The interim order  suspending the Presidential  pardon will  remain  effective until the final determination of the three FR petitions.

Pursuant to the  Interim order, the  Supreme  Court ordered   the CID to place Duminda Silva under the custody of  the  Commissioner -General of Prisons  with immediate effect.The Attorney General was directed to assist the CID to implement this interim order while reporting to court the progress of the implementation of the Interim Order.The   Supreme Court also issued an Interim Order impounding Duminda Silva’s passport.

Attorney – General Sanjay Rajaratnam wrote to the Inspector General of Police (IGP) informing him of the SC’s interim orders which also included a directive  preventing Duminda Silva from leaving the country and impounding his passport.The AG informed the IGP that the interim orders be enforced through the Director of the Criminal Investigation Department.

Meanwhile the Chairman of the National Housing Development Authority, Duminda Silva  had been admiited  to the Sri Jayewardenepura hospital for medical treatment. Consequent to the SC interim order, the CID took him  arrested  Silva at the Neurological ward of the hospital. He was placed under special guard. Subsequently  Duminda Silva was transported to Prison.

Attorney-at-law Eraj De Silva with Daminda Wijerathne and Sundramoorthy Janagan under the instructions of Attorney-at-law Dimuthu Kuruppuarachchi appeared for Sumana Premachandra. M.A. Sumanthiran PC appeared for Hirunika Premachandra  while Jeffry Alagaratnam PC Ghazali Hussain.

Additional Solicitor General Nerin Pulle appeared for the Attorney General. President’s Counsel Gamini Marapana with Navin Marapana PC appeared for Duminda Silva. K. Kanag Iswaran PC instructed by Senior Counsel G.G. Arulpragasam appeared for the Bar Association of Sri Lanka, a respondent party to the petitions.

The SC interim order has been hailed widely as a landmark ruling. Although an interim order, the Supreme Court has through  the ruling ,   called into question, the Constitutional prerogative of the Executive  President  in granting  a pardon. The AG’s position that the court has no dominion over the grant of a pardon, does not seem to  have  resulted in the customary exercise of judicial restraint. In that sense,the SC ruling is truly  a historic landmark. However it is only an interim order  for now and what transpires in the future  – as further hearings  continue – remains to be seen.

The interim order resulting in the suspension of the presidential pardon and re-imprisonment of Duminda Silva has once again turned the media spotlight on the chief victim   Bharatha Lakshman’s  courageous daughter Hirunika Premachandra. The former MP has earned the sympathy and admiration of many people by her doughty campaign  for justice and against injustice.  Although the “Gota Go Home”protest has spread far and wide in recent times,it must be remembered that it was Hirunika along with brave women comrades who started it all.They took the fight into the lion’s den by pioneering the protest demonstration at the President’s private residence in Mirihana.

When President Gotabaya Rajapaksa pardoned her father’s convicted killer on  Poson Poya day last year, Hirunika Premachandra wrote an emotional letter in Sinhala  to the executive president.  The “Colombo Telegraph” website published an English translation. The insightful letter  encapsules the essence of what happened before and after  the killing of her father.More importantly, it is somewhat prophetic  when viewed against the backdrop  of the current anti-Rajapaksa agitation. I shall conclude this article by re-producing that letter in full –
President of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka Presidential Secretariat
Re: Granting Presidential Pardon to R. Dumunda Silva, who was convicted for the murder of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra  by seven
I am the daughter of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra, Trade Union Adviser to your brother, former President Mahinda Rajapaksa, and former Member of Parliament.

On October 8, 2011, my beloved father was shot and killed in broad daylight, his body riddled with over 20 bullets. Two High Court judges convicted former MP R Duminda Silva of his murder, and their ruling was upheld by five judges of the Supreme Court, including the Chief Justice. Today, you granted him a presidential pardon.

On that fateful day, my mother and I received an anonymous telephone call informing us that my father had been shot. We did not panic initially, because we were used such phone calls. In the past they had always been followed up with confirmation that my father was safe from harm.

In 1999, it was my father who organized the final election rally for President Chandrika Bandaranaike Kumaratunga in Borella. After the LTTE bomb attack on that rally, my father reached out to our family on the telephone to confirm that he was unharmed in the blast. Every morning, my brother and I would worship our father before leaving for school. I vividly remember that when we walked up to him to pay our respects the morning after the bomb blast at the rally, his tunic was still smeared with blood.

In a war-torn country, these incidents were common during his political career that spanned 30 years. But that day in October 2011, the phone never stopped ringing. My mother and I decided to leave for the National Hospital in Colombo. When we got there, we were told to make our way to the mortuary.
Ten years have passed, and the memory of my father’s tragic death still crushes me, filling me with dread and making me feel like the whole world is collapsing around me.

I was 23 years old when my father was assassinated. My mother and I, who had never stepped into a courtroom or a police station during his lifetime, trekked from courtroom to courtroom for five long years, to seek justice for my father.

The 20s in any young girl’s life, is a time for happiness and playfulness. I spent those years locking horns with a group of powerful, brutal murderers. When he died, hardly anybody knew Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra had a daughter. After his death, the whole country knew my name overnight. I made terrible enemies. All because I decided to pursue justice in court against my father’s killers.

When the Rajapaksa regime was in power, Duminda Silva did not spend a second in jail. This was the man the highest court has said led my father’s assassins and murdered people in broad daylight. It was the CID Director Shani Abeysekera and his officers who conducted meticulous investigations into my father’s murder, collecting every shred of evidence in an orderly and unimpeachable way. Today, Shani Abeysekera suffers for his courage to execute his duties as a police officer independently.
From the moment my father was killed, politicians across party lines flocked to pay their final respects to him. But you never showed up.

Mahinda Rajapaksa, Chamal Rajapaksa, Basil Rajapaksa, Shiranthi Rajapaksa, Namal Rajapaksa, Yoshitha Rajapaksa and Rohitha Rajapaksa all your family members attended my father’s funeral. But still you never appeared. You remained at Duminda Silva’s bedside at the Sri Jayewardenepura Hospital. Your priority was to order a helicopter to fly Duminda Silva to Singapore.

Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra entered politics in 1977, under the guidance of the late T.B. Illangaratne. Subsequently, he engaged in political activities with Vijaya Kumaratunga, as a founding member of Sri Lanka Mahajana Pakshaya. Entering parliament as a member representing the Sri Lanka Mahajana Pakshaya in 1994, he became a member of the Sri Lanka Freedom Party, under President Chandrika Bandaranaike Kumaratunga in 1996. This was at a time when barely a handful of people could be assembled to hoist a blue flag. At times, he fought single-handedly for the SLFP in the Colombo District.

From the day they met, my father developed a close friendship with your brother Mahinda Rajapaksa. For this reason, my father would refer to him only as “Mahinda aiyya”. Every step of the way, through joy and sorrow, my father stood with Mahinda Rajapaksa.

It was my father who battled against President Chandrika Kumaratunge’s decision to give the post of Leader of the Opposition to another person. It was Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra who fought for Mahinda Rajapaksa to be appointed prime minister when the leadership had decided otherwise. Any current politician in the SLPP who once held membership in the SLFP, knows this story.

You can ask anyone of them, irrespective if they are parliamentarians or not. You can learn this history from the Prime Minister himself. He was completely dedicated to his friend Mahinda who he considered a brother. Where he believed he was standing on principle, my father would not budge. He raised his voice continuously for laborers and marginalized people at the grassroots level. He did not amass wealth as a politician. Instead, he acted as a friend and brother to everyone in the government and opposition.
But he made one misstep on a public stage a few days before he was killed.

“The Organizer of Kolonnawa, is taking the Defense Secretary who is the President’s brother, to every house to which he is selling Heroin. This is my worry. I must state at this point that we will not only ensure the victory of Prasanna. We will also fight to sever the Rajapaksa generation we built with much difficulty, from these heroin dealers.”
I believe that these few words may have led to his murder. These are the very last words my father uttered in public.
Mr. President, ask yourself how prophetic these words of a murdered man have proved today.

A decade later, my father’s brutal death still haunts me. From time to time, I have had to seek treatment to deal with the pain and trauma. As a mother of three, I try hard to be optimistic and move forward. But to this day, the loss of my father cuts through that joy and fills me with unbearable pain. Perhaps this is how all families  suffer the sudden, tragic death of a loved one. The families of Prageeth Eknaligoda, Lasantha Wickrematunge and Wasim Thajudeen – they must all be living through this trauma every day of their lives. No matter how full our lives are, what blessings we receive, there is a void in our hearts that will never be filled.
These victims and their families have never been given justice. They have a right to see these killers brought to court and face up to their crimes.
You campaigned and won the presidential election under the these ‘One Country, One Law’. Today, those words have been reduced to nothing more than sloganeering to win an election.

Now, if every prisoner on death row demands a presidential pardon, you are in no position to refuse. If convicted prisoners are to be arbitrarily released by presidential pardon, what are prisons for? Why do we have laws? Why do we have judges?

By your cowardly decisions, today, this country is lawless. This is a nation of slaves, in the grip of a master leading them to certain death. You are not the real ruler of this country today. You must accept that. You are just a puppet manipulated by other forces.

Believing in the vision of you as a leader like Mahathir Mohamed, Lee Kuan Yew or Vladimir Putin, 6.9 million Sri Lankan citizens placed their faith in you and propelled you to the presidency. They gave you a two thirds majority in Parliament. They made it possible for you to enact the 20th Amendment to the Constitution.

What more tools could you possibly need to make difficult decisions that will benefit all Sri Lankans. Instead, you have chosen to become a puppet. It is impossible to imagine now that your rule will ever usher in a better country. Your actions in this past year have amply demonstrated that.

My father is now a footnote in history.  But he has left behind so much for his grandchildren to take pride in and remember. What legacy do you hope to leave behind? What will  you leave behind for your newborn granddaughter to speak of proudly when you are gone?

In accordance with the Buddhist precepts that I live by, I have already forgiven former MP Duminda Silva. But Buddhism also teaches that rulers must be just and uphold the laws of the land. Your actions have undermined the rule of law and made a mockery of justice.

Two out of three High Court judges ruled that Duminda Silva had murdered my father and sentenced the killers to death. The defendants appealed to the Supreme Court. Five judges of the Supreme Court including the then Chief Justice, upheld the verdict of the High Court. Five judges of the Supreme Court determined that Duminda Silva had been the ringleader of the illegal assembly that killed my father. If the decisions of High Court and Supreme Court judges are cast into the garbage and the final decisions are made by you, of what use are laws in this country?

6.9 million people put their faith in you and chose you to be leader of this country. You must now be prepared to answer them. Your present conduct makes sure that the people will never allow a Rajapaksa to rule the country again.

Now that you have dismissed the verdict of seven judges to release Duminda Silva, I would not be surprised if you were to nominate him as a Chief Minister and bring him back into politics. This country is transformed now into a nation of slaves, being marshalled towards their own destruction by their masters. But the people will not tolerate injustice forever.

Your rule is breeding injustice, Mr President, and the day is not far off when the people will break these shackles and rise up. When it comes, people like you who have no backbone and no respect for justice and the rule of law will be bereft of a political future.
